Peking University Cancer Hospital

Peking University Cancer Hospital

BEIJING CANCER HOSPITAL
Gender: Male
Age: 62 years old
Department: Thoracic Oncology Department I
Date of Admission: 2023-09-27
Date of Discharge: 2023-09-29
Unit: Huairou District Education Committee
Summary of Condition and Diagnosis:
The patient was admitted to the hospital because he was diagnosed with right lung small cell carcinoma about two months ago. The patient's first pathological diagnosis was on 2020-06-17, and genetic testing showed FGFR positive on 2021-01-07. A comprehensive routine examination was performed before admission, and Chief Physician Zhao Jun reviewed and formulated a treatment plan. The patient is currently in the cT1cN1Mlc IVB stage of right lung small cell carcinoma, i.e., the extensive stage. The treatment plan includes etoposide, carboplatin and slulizumab. After checking for contraindications to treatment, the C3 stage of etoposide, carboplatin and slulizumab therapy was started on September 27, 2023. The specific drug doses were etoposide 100mg/m2, 200mg d1-3; carboplatin AUC=5 600mg d1; slulizumab 4.5mg/kg 400mg d1, one cycle every 21 days. Supportive treatments such as antiemetic, liver protection, and stomach protection were given during the treatment, and the treatment of this cycle was successfully completed. The patient is in good general condition and can be discharged with the approval of the superior physician.
Treatment recommendations:
1. Use long-acting white blood cell injections after 48 hours, and regularly perform blood routine and liver and kidney function tests (1-2 times/week). If the test results are abnormal, please go to the outpatient clinic in time;
2. The next cycle (cycle 4) of treatment is expected to start on October 18, 2023. Please contact the hospital to reserve a bed 7 days before treatment, and go through the hospitalization procedures in the outpatient department in advance. When you are admitted to the hospital, you need to provide blood routine, liver and kidney function test results within the past 3 days;
3. Perform an effect evaluation after every two treatment cycles, including chest and abdominal enhanced CT, ultrasound examination of superficial lymph nodes and tumor marker tests;
4. Pay attention to rest, prevent infection, and seek medical attention immediately if you feel uncomfortable.
Discharge diagnosis: right lung small cell carcinoma cT1cN1Mlc IVB stage extensive stage, right hilar lymph node metastasis, lumbar 4 vertebral and left iliac bone metastasis, hepatitis B virus, unstable angina, hypertension grade 3 (extremely high risk), constipation

Main complaint: Diagnosed with left lung cancer for more than 1 year, after multiple courses of treatment.
Medical history narrator:
Patient himself
Admission: Intermittent needle-like pain in the left chest appeared in early September 2020, the degree was mild, did not affect sleep and rest, no fever, sputum blood, chest pain, etc., the pain worsened at the end of November 2020, and the local hospital for chest CT examination showed solid nodules in the left upper lobe of the lung and diffuse small nodules in both lungs. Medical data showed that the NTRK3 gene was abnormal, the MET gene had a MET14 deletion mutation, the disease name was invasive mucinous adenocarcinoma, the KRAS gene mutation was G13D, and the brain metastasis date was 2021-03-06. On December 4, PET-CT was performed in our hospital in the Gene Village. It showed: a solid mass in the left upper lobe of the lung, accompanied by increased metabolism, which was considered lung cancer; multiple nodules in the remaining lungs and pleural surfaces, accompanied by increased metabolism, and considered intrapulmonary metastasis; multiple thickening of the left pleura with increased metabolism, considered metastasis; increased metabolism in the strip of the left lower chest wall, the nature of which was to be determined; multiple lymph nodes in the mediastinum (4L, 5, 7 areas), left hilum, and left pericardial septum group, accompanied by increased metabolism, considered metastasis; small lymph nodes in the mediastinum 4R, 6 areas, accompanied by mild increased metabolism, tending to be mainly granulomatous lesions; bilateral lock Small lymph nodes scattered in the supraspinal area and deep cervical group, some with mild metabolic activity, the nature of which is to be determined; metastasis to the left 6th rib and lumbar vertebral body, with increased metabolism; local density of the sacrum decreased, focal metabolism increased in the right wing of the sacrum, also tending to be metastatic; diffuse metabolism increased in the middle and upper rectum, poor local intestinal expansion, slight thickening of the intestinal wall, the nature of which is to be determined; multiple lymph nodes around the intestinal tract and in the presacral area, with mild increased metabolism, the nature of which is to be determined; multiple lymph nodes in the retroperitoneum, some with mild increased metabolism; thickening of the left adrenal medial branch, with increased metabolism, alert for metastasis. Color Doppler ultrasound-guided puncture of the left supraclavicular lymph node; pathology showed a small number of adenocarcinoma cells, but immunohistochemistry and gene testing were not performed due to too little tissue. Adenocarcinoma cells were found in the left upper lobe of the lung under CT guidance. On December 14, 2020 and January 4, 2021, the first and second cycles of pemetrexed + cisplatin chemotherapy were given respectively (drugs: pemetrexed 1.0g, did cisplatin 50mg, d1-3, Q21d), and zoledronic acid treatment was given. Genetic testing report on January 4, 2021: showed deletion mutation of exon 19 of EGFR gene. On January 28, 2021, due to the epidemic, he was unable to return to Beijing, and received the third cycle of pemetrexed + cisplatin chemotherapy (specifically pemetrexed disodium 1000mg d1 + cisplatin 50mg d1-3) at a local hospital, and began oral targeted therapy with icotinib hydrochloride (125mg tid) on February 5. On March 26, 2021, our hospital received the fourth cycle of pemetrexed + cisplatin chemotherapy (specifically pemetrexed disodium 1000mg d1 + cisplatin 50mg d1-3 treatment), and gave zoledronic acid treatment, with no obvious discomfort. The efficacy evaluation of 3 cycles was PR. On March 19, 2021, our hospital received the fifth cycle of treatment: pemetrexed disodium 10g, d1\\
cisplatin 50mg, d1-3, ivgtt, Q21, icotinib 125mg tid, and zoledronic acid treatment. After chemotherapy, the patient had obvious vomiting and fatigue, and grade III bone marrow suppression, which improved after symptomatic treatment and maintained PR after 5 cycles of efficacy evaluation. The patient had bilateral lower limb eyelid edema after angiography, and improved after anti-allergic treatment. The patient had obvious nausea and vomiting after 5 cycles of chemotherapy. On April 13, 2021, the sixth cycle of chemotherapy was performed, and the regimen was adjusted to: pemetrexed disodium 1.0g, d1+carboplatin 500mg, d2, ivgtt, Q21, icotinib 125mg tid. The patient still complained of obvious nausea and vomiting after 6 cycles of chemotherapy, so the regimen was adjusted on May 7, 2021: pemetrexed disodium lg, ivgtt, d1, Q21d, icotinib 125mg, po, tid/Q3W. On May 28, 2021, the original regimen was continued for the eighth cycle of treatment. On June 25, 2021, targeted therapy with bevacizumab combined with icotinib was started: bevacizumab 500mg
ivgtt d1/Q28d + icotinib 125mg tid. The efficacy of the CT reexamination during the period was evaluated as SD. On June 14, 2022, CT: solid tumor in the left upper lobe of the lung, with extremely irregular shape, some layers are slightly fuller than before, and the current size is about 3.4×2.4cm, with multiple burrs on the edge and more blurred than before, and multiple metastatic nodules in both lungs and pleura, some of which are similar to the previous ones, and some are slightly larger than before. Considering the progression of the disease, the treatment plan was changed on June 20, 2022: pemetrexed 1.0g dl + bevacizumab 500mg dl + osimertinib 80mg qd po/Q28d. On February 10, 2023, it was adjusted to pemetrexed + anlotinib + osimertinib treatment. 2023.5.9 Due to intolerance to adverse reactions, pemetrexed was suspended. 2023. 07.10 The treatment plan was adjusted to bevacizumab + albumin paclitaxel + carboplatin. 
Past history: denied hepatitis, tuberculosis and other infectious diseases, denied hypertension, denied coronary heart disease, denied diabetes and other chronic diseases, denied

Please consult with the Department of Thoracic Surgery and perform ultrasound combined with CT-guided percutaneous lung puncture biopsy (Consultation record of thoracic surgery: The nature of the patient's left lower lobe lesion was unclear, so ultrasound combined with CT-guided percutaneous lung puncture biopsy was performed. The process was as follows: supine position, CT confirmed the puncture point, local disinfection and drape, 2% lidocaine 10ml local anesthesia, and under ultrasound guidance, the coaxial puncture needle was inserted into the chest wall as planned to reach the parietal pleura, paying attention to avoiding the intercostal vessels. After sufficient anesthesia of the pleura, it was inserted into the tumor as planned. CT confirmed that the position was appropriate. Four biopsy needles were sent for examination. The operation was completed and went smoothly. The patient did not have hemoptysis, chest tightness, shortness of breath or discomfort during and after the operation. No pneumothorax was found on the postoperative CT. Anti-inflammatory treatment was given after the operation. If necessary, the chest X-ray was repeated to confirm whether there was pneumothorax. Surgeon: Sheng Dongsheng). Hemoptysis occurred after the operation, the amount was about 50mL, and there was slight shortness of breath. It was relieved after oxygen inhalation, cough suppressants, anti-inflammatory and hemostatic symptomatic treatment. The patient currently has a large tumor burden. Combined with the characteristics of tumor markers, it is considered that the possibility of left lung adenocarcinoma is high. Female non-smoking adenocarcinoma patients are the EGFR mutation-dominant population. Ametinib targeted therapy is given while waiting for the pathological results and genetic test results. The patient and his family members requested to recuperate outside the hospital. They were told to take relevant precautions and consulted with the superior physician before being discharged today.

Date of discharge: 2023-07-10

Summary of condition and diagnosis:
The patient had a postoperative recurrence of left lung adenocarcinoma. The first pathological diagnosis was confirmed on 2020-08-27. The RET gene was positive, thymic carcinoma was present, and KEAP1 was positive. This admission was for SRS treatment of intracranial metastases. The treatment plan was GTV, cerebellar metastases, 24Gy/1 time; 95% PTV, cerebellar metastases with a three-dimensional expansion of 2mm, 22Gy/1 time. At discharge, the diagnosis was M1c IVB stage, brain metastases, renal metastases, and combined with grade 3 hypertension.
Treatment recommendations:
1. Adjust your diet, increase nutritional intake, ensure that you take in high-quality protein and enough calories, and avoid catching cold;
2. Check blood routine and liver and kidney function every week, and seek medical attention in time if there are any abnormalities;
3. It is recommended to receive bevacizumab treatment in the outpatient day ward, with a dose of 7.5mg/kg, once every 21 days, for a total of 4 times, to prevent radiation brain necrosis, check urine routine every cycle, and pay attention to monitoring blood pressure changes;
4. If symptoms such as headache, nausea and vomiting or limb movement disorders occur, seek medical attention in time.

N1Mx. The patient was in good general condition, with stable vital signs and no fever. No obvious complaints of discomfort. input: In the discharge record on October 31, 2023, the patient was treated in the outpatient department of Peking University Cancer Hospital, and the main reason for the visit was lung adenocarcinoma. According to the medical history, the patient was found to have a lung mass with enlarged lymph nodes in October 2023. After a lymph node puncture biopsy, the pathological results showed that cancer cells were visible in the left cervical lymph nodes and the left supraclavicular lymph nodes, combined with immunohistochemistry, suggesting lung adenocarcinoma metastasis. Subsequently, the patient underwent a PET-CT examination, which showed irregular soft tissue density foci in the posterior segment of the left upper lobe apex and multiple lymph node metastases. The patient's past history includes a 20-year history of smoking, no history of drinking, and no other obvious history of disease. Physical examination showed that the ECOG score was 0. The outpatient diagnosis was left lung adenocarcinoma and secondary malignant tumors of the lymph nodes. The doctor recommended additional genetic testing and PD-L1 expression testing, and oral folic acid 400ug qd. At the same time, according to medical data, the patient had hemothorax and positive KEAP1 gene expression, the first symptom time was February 16, 2021, the first imaging diagnosis time was July 14, 2020, and the ECOG score was recorded on October 19, 2021. In addition, the patient also suffered from hepatitis B infectious diseases. JSON results:

instruction: Your task is to extract medical information from the input report and output it in JSON format, and output NA for information not mentioned in the report
input:
The basic information of the patient showed that he was 73 years old and had a 40-year history of smoking, but had quit smoking for 5 years. The patient was diagnosed with squamous cell lung carcinoma, with a clinical stage of cT3N1M1a, belonging to stage IVA. The diagnosis of lung cancer was on October 23, 2010. Bronchoscopy showed that the patient had lesions such as pulmonary fibrosis, emphysema, and bullae. In addition, the patient had a history of coronary heart disease and had two stents implanted 18 years ago. He also had chronic atrophic gastritis, gastric ulcer, and chronic appendicitis. The patient usually took aspirin, atorvastatin, bisoprolol fumarate, levamlodipine besylate, and other drugs.

Immunohistochemistry results showed that the tumor was moderately differentiated, P40 positive, CEA negative, TTF-1 negative, P53 mutant, and Ki-67 positive. Genetic testing showed that the STK11 gene had a c.920+1G>T mutation, accounting for 47.1%, the TP53 gene had a p.R175H mutation, accounting for 30.1%, and the TP53 gene had a p.R213* mutation, accounting for 2.6%. The PD-L1 expression level was low, and both TPS and CPS were less than 1. Immunoexpression showed that TMBH was 12.0Muts/Mb, which was a microsatellite stable type.

Tumor imaging showed that the tumor was in the dorsal segment of the left lower lobe, with an SUVmax value of 27.6. PET-CT examination showed that there were multiple lymph node metastases in the left hilar area, under the tracheal carina, and at the lumbar 5 plane. input:
The admission and discharge records are updated as follows:

Basic information
The patient is a 73-year-old male with a 40-year history of smoking and has quit smoking for 5 years. After admission, he was diagnosed with squamous cell lung carcinoma, with a clinical stage of cT3N1M1a, belonging to stage IVA.

Diagnosis time
The patient underwent bronchoscopy on October 23, 2010, and squamous cell lung carcinoma was found, accompanied by pulmonary fibrosis, emphysema and bullae. He had coronary heart disease before, and had two stents implanted 18 years ago. He also suffered from chronic atrophic gastritis, gastric ulcer and chronic appendicitis.

Daily medication
The patient is currently being treated with aspirin, atorvastatin, bisoprolol fumarate and amlodipine besylate.

Tumor pathological characteristics
According to the results of immunohistochemistry, the tumor is a moderately differentiated squamous cell carcinoma, which shows P40 and PCK positive, TTF-1, INSM1, and NapsinA negative, and the Ki-67 positive rate is 80%. Genetic testing results showed that the STK11 gene had c.920+1G>T mutations, and the TP53 gene had p.R175H and p.R213* mutations.

Immune expression and PD-L1 detection
The TPS and CPS of PD-L1 were both <1%, and immune expression showed that TMBH was 12.0Muts/Mb, which was a microsatellite stable type.

Tumor imaging and treatment plan
The tumor imaging showed that the SUVmax value was 27.6 in the dorsal segment of the left lower lobe. Subsequently, a single chemotherapy treatment plan of white purple + cisplatin was performed.

Digestive system diseases and other test results
According to the latest medical data, the patient also had ROS1 gene mutations (S1986F, G3032R), HER4 (ERBB4) positive expression, KEAP1 positive expression, TP53 positive expression, and a history of digestive system diseases such as esophageal varices. json results:

instruction: Your task is to extract medical information from the input report and output it in JSON format, and output NA for information not mentioned in the report
input:
According to medical data, the patient Lei Guiqin suffered from right lung adenocarcinoma and had undergone liver cancer resection surgery on 2021-01-25. The first symptoms appeared on 2021-04-26. The HER4 (ERBB4) test result was positive, and the RET test result showed the presence of fusion. She also had a history of liver cancer and nasopharyngeal carcinoma.

In the body of the report, existing studies have shown that patients with positive MRD (molecular residual disease) test in peripheral blood after treatment have a poor prognosis. In a clinical study of stage I-llI lung cancer, a total of 40 patients were included, including 33 NSCLC patients, 7 SCLC patients, 7 stage IB patients, and 33 stage II-III patients. Plasma ctDNA was tested at multiple follow-up nodes in 37 patients after treatment, of which 20 patients tested positive for ctDNA at least in one follow-up node, and all of these patients relapsed. Statistics of 32 patients with first-node plasma samples within 4 months after treatment showed a ctDNA detection rate of 53%, a 36-month free progression rate (FFP) of 0% in the ctDNA-positive group, and a 36-month free progression rate (FFP) of 93% in the ctDNA-negative group, with a hazard ratio of 43.4.

In addition, a clinical study (TRACERx) for early non-small cell lung cancer showed that a total of 24 patients were included in the study of the predictive value of postoperative ctDNA detection for recurrence risk, of which the recurrence rate in the ctDNA-positive group was 93% and the recurrence rate in the ctDNA-negative group was 10%. Dynamic detection of ctDNA can analyze the subclonal evolution of recurrent NSCLC, evaluate the response to adjuvant therapy, and provide guidance for personalized treatment before clinical recurrence.

In summary, MRD detection is of great significance in the prognosis evaluation and personalized treatment of lung cancer patients, and can guide the consolidation of treatment strategies and improve the treatment effect of patients. The detection results of MRD should be considered in combination with the specific clinical conditions of the patient, and should not be used as the sole basis for guiding clinical decision-making. json results:

Jingxi Cancer Hospital
Beijing Jingxi Cancer Hospital
Outpatient Comprehensive Medical Record
Print: 2023-10-27 09:10
Revision Date: 2023-10-27
Surname Main Disease
Gender: Female
Age: 82 years old
Date of Birth: 1964-07-26
No.: 20231027-75713-1
Complaint: Diagnosis of right lung cancer stage IV bone metastasis for nearly 7 years, brain metastasis for more than 3 years targeted treatment
Medical History: The patient has coughed and coughed up blood since September 2016. Gray-white tissue with a diameter of about 0.2cm was found in the right upper lobe of the lung. It was diagnosed as lung adenocarcinoma, mainly solid growth. A high metabolic mass appeared in the right upper lobe of the lung, which grew outward across the oblique fissure. It was considered to be lung cancer, accompanied by atelectasis. Multiple lymph node metastases in the mediastinum 2R, 3P, and 7 groups, and right pleural effusion. On October 14, 2016, genetic testing showed exon 21 mutations, and targeted drug Conmena could be used for treatment. After one month of treatment, the tumor shrank from 6 cm to 4 cm, but liver function was impaired. Bone metastasis treatment was performed on May 16, 2018, and targeted therapy was continued. No new lesions were found in the reexamination on May 30, 2018. On June 21, 2019, a chest CT scan in our hospital showed that the soft tissue mass in the right upper lobe of the lung was slightly larger than before, the right pleural effusion was slightly reduced, and there were multiple bone destructions in the thoracic spine and bilateral ribs, which were considered to be metastases. There were multiple low-enhancement nodules in the spleen, the tumor marker CEA increased slightly, and CA125 and CA199 decreased slightly. The patient was in good general condition and had no discomfort. On July 9, 2019, a cranial MRI was performed at Beijing Chaoyang Hospital Affiliated to Capital Medical University, showing lesions in the right parietal lobe and left frontal lobe. Combined with the medical history, it was considered to be a metastatic tumor. On December 25, 2019, local radiotherapy was started in the radiotherapy department, SBRT 95% PGTV1, 3mm three-dimensional expansion of the left frontal lobe lesion, 28Gy/2 times; 95% PGTV2, 3mm three-dimensional expansion of the right occipital lobe lesion, 52Gy/8 times. Brain radiotherapy ended on October 6, 2020. Treatment ended on April 12, 2021. CT examination on April 17, 2020 showed that the right upper lobe cancer, a small amount of pleural effusion on the right side, and scattered small patchy nodules in both lungs were not significantly changed compared with the previous changes; multiple metastatic destruction of the thoracic spine and bilateral ribs, some of which were new; diffuse low-density lesions in the spleen were scanned. On April 21, 2020, the head MRI showed that the structures of the bilateral cerebral hemispheres were roughly symmetrical, and double abnormal enhancement nodules of about 6mm were seen in the left frontal lobe and right parietal lobe, with flake edema areas around the lesions; double abnormal enhancement nodules with edema in the left frontal lobe and right parietal lobe were considered metastasis. The brain MRI on August 27, 2020 showed that the metastatic nodules in the left frontal lobe and right parietal lobe were slightly enlarged (increased by 2mm), and the peritumoral edema was slightly more obvious than before. The right frontal lobe enhancement focus was also considered a metastatic focus. The comparison between the brain MRI on November 30, 2020 and the head MRI on August 27, 2020 showed that the bilateral abnormal enhancement nodules in the frontal lobe and right parietal lobe were enlarged, the larger one was about 8mm, and now it is about 9x7mm. The brain MRI on June 15, 2021 showed that the multiple metastases in the brain were enlarged, and the range of peritumoral edema was enlarged. The patient had no symptoms related to cranial and brain metastasis, and the patient refused to actively undergo anti-tumor treatment. The head CT on October 18, 2021 showed that the multiple metastases in the brain were enlarged, and the range of peritumoral edema was enlarged. Chest CT showed that the mass in the right upper lobe of the lung was the same as before, and a small amount of pleural effusion on the right side was the same as before. The scattered nodules in both lungs were roughly the same as before: multiple bone metastases and pathological compression fractures of thoracic vertebrae 5-7 were the same as before. Multiple nodular high-density lesions beside the right humeral head are the same as before. Splenic diffuse low-density lesions were also found. A reexamination of the head MRI in February 2023 showed that the brain edema was aggravated compared with the previous one. Reexamination and evaluation on April 28, 2023: The brain lesions were reduced. Outpatient evaluation on August 4, 2023: The brain edema worsened and the right lung pleural effusion increased. Oral vometinib has been continued until now. Reexamination of chest enhanced CT and brain MRI on October 17, 2023 showed that the lung and brain lesions were unchanged compared with the previous ones. Consult for follow-up treatment

2. History of infectious diseases: The patient was infected with Treponema pallidum.

4. Treatment process:
- Disease progression was evaluated on August 8, 2023.
- The first cycle of chemotherapy was performed on August 9, 2023.
- The second cycle of chemotherapy was performed on August 30, 2023. During this period, the white blood cell count decreased, and it improved after the injection of white blood cell injection.
- The patient was seen on September 6, 2023, and re-examined on September 22. After chemotherapy, the patient developed insomnia, difficulty falling asleep, dreaming and nightmares, general discomfort, fatigue and numbness of the limbs.
- On October 20, 2023, four cycles of paclitaxel treatment were performed.
- On October 25, 2023, ultrasound examination showed bilateral cervical lymph nodes, and continued observation; fatty liver and slightly enlarged spleen.
- On October 30, 2023, CT showed that compared with the chest CT on September 14, there was no change in the nodules in the left lower lobe of the lung; right lung carcinomatous lymphangitis was aggravated, and there was no change in the extensive metastatic nodules in both lungs; there was no change in the multiple lymph nodes in the mediastinum, bilateral hilar and supraclavicular regions, and there was no change in the small amount of pericardial effusion.

5. Last visit:
- On November 2, 2023, the imaging results were consulted, and the patient showed peripheral neuritis and weakness in both lower limbs. Psychological tests showed severe anxiety, somatization symptoms, mild to moderate depression, and poor sleep quality.

Outpatient diagnosis:
1. Cough (brain metastasis of non-small cell lung cancer EGFR 19 exon mutation)
2. Peripheral neuritis
3. Insomnia with anxiety and depression

Treatment suggestions:
- Continue relevant treatment and monitor the patient's condition.
- Strengthen psychological support and sleep management.

Date of admission: 2023-10-18 08:06:00
Ethnicity: Han
Record date: 2023-10-18 10:00:00
Main complaint: diagnosed with lung cancer for more than 5 months, 6 cycles of chemotherapy
Current medical history: The patient had intermittent cough without obvious cause from March 2023 to March 2024, mainly dry cough, occasionally coughing up a little white sputum, no fever, no hemoptysis, no chest pain, coughing at night, no general fatigue, no muscle aches and joint pains. The complete lung CT in our outpatient clinic showed: space-occupying lesions in the left lower lung with multiple nodules in both lungs and space-occupying left adrenal gland. Routine blood tests showed no abnormalities. The patient went to the respiratory department of our hospital for a fiber bronchial pathological biopsy. The pathology showed: basal segment of the left lower lobe, adenocarcinoma, and the results of genetic testing showed: BRCA positive, EGFR mutations including G719D and 20 insertions, HER2 (ERBB2) positive, TP53 positive. The PET-CT examination in our hospital showed: the FDG metabolism of the space-occupying lesion in the left lower lobe of the lung was significantly increased, which was consistent with the characteristics of malignant transformation. The possibility of left lung cancer was considered high. Please combine the clinical and pathological results. The FDG metabolism of multiple nodules in both lungs was abnormally increased, and multiple metastatic tumors in both lungs were considered. The left adrenal gland mass was accompanied by a slight increase in FDG metabolism, and follow-up was recommended. The bilateral cervical lymph nodes were enlarged, and some FDG metabolism was increased; the FDG metabolism of small lymph nodes in the mediastinum, bilateral axilla, pelvic cavity, and bilateral inguinal area was not significantly increased, combined with clinical data. On 2023-04-20 and 2023-05-11, the patient underwent pemetrexed 0.8g ivgtt + carboplatin injection 500mg ivgtt + bevacizumab 700 ivgtt q21d in our department. On 2023-05-31, the ALT was 266 U/L and AST 141U/L at Daqing City Traditional Chinese Medicine Hospital. He received liver protection treatment in our hospital. Then on 2023-06-06, 07-20, and 2023-08-09, he continued to receive 6 cycles of chemotherapy with the original regimen, and then received 2 cycles of bevacizumab maintenance treatment. The patient's chest CT examination showed progression compared with the previous one, and the efficacy evaluation was PD. The patient went to our outpatient clinic for further treatment. After reading the relevant information, the outpatient physician admitted the patient to our department with "lung malignancy". During the course of the disease, the patient had no dizziness, headache, nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, abdominal distension, abdominal pain, frequent urination, urgency, or pain, no change in urine color, good diet, good sleep, normal bowel movements, and no weight change.

Condition at admission: The patient has no fever, no cough, no dyspnea or wheezing, no nausea or vomiting, no headache or dizziness, no frequent urination or urgency, good eating and sleeping, and normal urine and stool. Physical examination: General condition is good, clear mind and clear speech, moderate nutrition, and no anemia appearance. The neck is soft, no enlarged lymph nodes are palpated on both sides of the clavicle, no cyanosis of the lips, the trachea is centered, no rales or wheezing are heard in both lungs, the heart sounds are pure, the heart rate is 77 beats/min, the rhythm is regular, and no pathological murmurs are heard. The abdomen is flat and soft, no gastrointestinal type and peristaltic waves are seen, no tenderness or rebound pain, the liver and spleen are not palpated, and the bowel sounds are 5 times/min. The upper limbs are well mobile, there is no clubbing of fingers and toes, and there is no edema in the lower limbs. The knee tendon reflex and abdominal wall reflex are symmetrical, and the K-sign and Babinski sign are negative.

Admission diagnosis: lung malignant tumor T3NOM1 IV stage secondary lung malignant tumor

Treatment process: The patient's condition has progressed. According to the NCCN guidelines, the second-line treatment can be docetaxel chemotherapy. The patient's blood routine and liver and kidney function have no obvious contraindications. Today, docetaxel 120mg ivgtt chemotherapy is given, supplemented by antiemetic, stomach protection and other relief treatments. The patient's treatment is over and he will be discharged today.

Discharge status: The patient currently has no fever, no cough, no dyspnea and wheezing, no nausea, vomiting, no headache, no dizziness, no frequent urination, no urgency, good eating and sleeping, and normal urine and stool. Physical examination: The general condition is good, the mind is clear and the speech is clear, the nutrition is moderate, and there is no anemia appearance. The neck was soft, no enlarged lymph nodes were palpated on both sides of the clavicle, no cyanosis of the lips, the trachea was centered, no rales or wheezes were heard in both lungs, the heart sounds were pure, the heart rate was 77 beats/min, the rhythm was regular, and no pathological murmurs were heard. The abdomen was flat and soft, no gastrointestinal type and peristaltic waves were seen, no tenderness and rebound pain, the liver and spleen were not palpable, and the bowel sounds were 5 times/min. The upper limbs were well active, there was no clubbing of the fingers and toes, and there was no edema in the lower limbs. The knee tendon reflex and abdominal wall reflex were symmetrical, and the K-sign and Babinski sign were negative.

Discharge diagnosis: lung malignant tumor T3NOM1 IV stage secondary lung malignant tumor

In addition, the patient had a report showing that he had his first lung surgery on 2021-01-07 and was diagnosed with brain metastasis on 2022-06-12. The patient's ECOG score date was 2021-03-04, indicating that the patient maintained a good functional status during treatment. json results:

2018-04-16 Lung puncture pathology: adenocarcinoma, ALK -, PD-L1 weak 5%. Staging examination was complete and no distant metastasis was found. Genetic testing (blood + tissue) EGFR 21 exon mutation, TP53 mutation.
On May 3, 2018, the patient started taking the third-generation osimertinib orally for one month, and the pleural effusion increased. Combined with bevacizumab, the patient was reexamined after two months of medication, and the lung lesions were relieved and the mediastinal lymph nodes improved.
On May 10, 2019, the first pathological diagnosis was confirmed as lung adenocarcinoma. The surgical site was lung resection, and the endocrine and immune system diagnosis was Guillain-Barré syndrome, and KRAS was confirmed to be positive and BRAF was not V600.
On September 5, 2018, the patient reported that he was currently using osimertinib + Avastin.
On September 3, 2019, chest CT: Multiple ground-glass density shadows in both lungs were roughly the same as before. The large area of ​​light shadows in the middle and upper lobes of the right lung was larger than before, and the multiple lymph nodes in the mediastinum group 4, right hilum and right axilla were similar to those before.
On September 3, 2019, he took osimertinib orally for 1 year and 5 months. In mid-September 2019, bevacizumab was combined. The right lung nodules were blurred and transaminase was elevated. The lesions were stable in August 2020. Oral osimertinib was continued, and 5 injections of bevacizumab were injected every 21 days. 2021-5-30 Color Doppler ultrasound in an external hospital: lymph nodes were visible on both sides of the neck; abdominal color doppler ultrasound: fatty liver, liver cysts; blood routine: WBC3.15×109/L. 2021-6-8 Chest CT of Nanjiao Cancer Hospital: multiple ground-glass shadows in both lungs, with lighter density; the cord shadows on the septum of the left lower lobe were the same as before, and the small nodules were the same as before; the right hilum, mediastinum, and right axillary small lymph nodes were roughly the same as before. 
The patient now has cough, intermittent nasal mucosal bleeding, occasional diarrhea, no bloody sputum, no nausea and vomiting, no dizziness and headache. 2021-9-28 After more than 3 years of oral osimertinib + Avastin treatment, the patient complained of occasional nasal mucosal bleeding and right sacral pain.
Chest CT: Multiple ground-glass shadows in both lungs are the same as before. The upper cord shadows and micronodules on the diaphragm of the left lower lobe are the same as before. 2022-03-16 Re-examination of chest enhanced CT: After right breast cancer surgery: no abnormalities were found in the right chest wall; the right hilar and mediastinal lymph nodes were slightly enlarged, and some were slightly larger than before; the pleura on both sides was thickened;

Beijing Jingxi Cancer Hospital
Outpatient Comprehensive Medical Record
IL
Print: 2023-11-03 12:33
Date of Visit: 2023-11-03
Gender: Male
Age: 66 years old
No.: 20231103-79123-1
Main complaint: Left upper lobe adenocarcinoma more than 5 years after surgery, chemotherapy and targeted therapy after recurrence and metastasis
Medical history: 2018-8 Left upper lobe resection was performed in another hospital, and postoperative pathology showed adenocarcinoma. 2018.10.25 Genetic testing showed an insertion mutation in EGFR exon 20. 2021/1/18 PET/CT showed: 1. No high metabolic lesions were found in the bronchial stump after left upper lobectomy. 2. Multiple small nodules were newly found in both lungs, with slightly increased metabolism, which may be bilateral lung metastasis. Multiple new lobular septal thickening was found in the right lung, and carcinomatous lymphangitis could not be ruled out. A small amount of effusion appeared in the bilateral chest cavity and pericardium. Metastatic lymph nodes were found in the 7th zone of the mediastinum and the right hilum. New bone metastasis appeared in the T11 vertebra. 2. No abnormal metabolic signs were found in the brain. Enhanced MR was recommended to exclude smaller metastatic lesions. 2021-2-4 Started to participate in the clinical trial (TAK788); (control group) received pemetrexed + carboplatin regimen for 4 cycles (last chemotherapy time 2021.04.09), and was evaluated as PR. 2021.04.27 The patient underwent pulmonary artery CT, which showed: pulmonary embolism, affecting the posterior and dorsal basal arteries of the right lower lung. Regular chest enhanced CT and anticoagulation treatment for pulmonary embolism were performed. Thrombolytic therapy was then performed; 2021-6-9 CT reexamination showed no pulmonary embolism. 2021-7-16 Reexamination of chest CT showed: some small nodules in both lungs were the same as before, and some were enlarged and increased. Thickening of the interlobular septa of both lungs. T11 high-density lesions were the same as before. In July 2021, chest CT showed that the nodules in the lungs increased and continued to be observed. On August 27, chest CT showed that some of the nodules in the lungs were the same as before, and some were enlarged. On September 16, head MRI showed new multiple brain metastases. On September 17, oral TAK788 was started, which caused high blood pressure. Oral metoprolol was started. A small light red rash appeared on the outer thigh and chest and back. ALT rose to 71 briefly, and then took liver protection drugs to return to normal. Back pain, acid reflux, oral ulcers, symptoms were slightly relieved after symptomatic treatment. On December 9, 2021, head MRI showed that the nodules in the left basal ganglia were larger than before. On December 8, 2021, chest CT showed that there were new small nodules in the right lower lobe of the lung, and some nodules in both lungs were enlarged. On December 17, 2021, outpatient clinic: the disease progressed, and it was recommended to stop using TAK788. On December 23, 2021, brain radiotherapy was started, a total of 10 times.
On January 17, 2022, the chest and abdominal CT scans showed new diffuse nodules in both lungs, new thickening of the interlobular septa in the right lung, small amounts of effusion in the bilateral pleural cavity and pericardium, and new bone metastasis to T11. The patient had no discomfort. Currently, targeted therapy is being performed with 240 mg of vometinib qd. ALT 2740 and AST 1440 were checked at present, indicating severe liver damage. The dose was adjusted to 80 mg qd until now. On July 20, 2022, the lung CT scans and head MRI scans showed stable condition. Oral vometinib was continued. After 3 months, the follow-up evaluation on October 26, 2022 showed disease progression. On October 28, 2022, vometinib 160 mg qd was started, and liver function was normal after medication. On July 7, 2023, the chest enhanced CT scans showed a decrease in pleural effusion, a slight increase in the small nodules in the right upper lung, and a slight increase in the range of metastatic tumors in the lumbar spine. Consult for follow-up treatment. On October 26, 2023, chest CT scan showed that the metastatic lesions in both lungs were slightly enlarged, the pleural effusion had no obvious changes, cancerous lymphangiopathy was possible, pathological compression fractures of T5 and T10 vertebrae were possible, and the cranial MRI showed that the brain metastasis was the same as before, and the treatment evaluation was slow progression. Past history: long-term hypertension, taking antihypertensive drugs, and had experienced liver failure in the past. He also suffered from digestive system diseases, specifically hiatal hernia. Diagnosis: left upper lobe adenocarcinoma, brain metastasis, lung metastasis, bone metastasis, EGFR20 positive, targeted therapy, insomnia and anxiety after pulmonary artery embolism treatment. Recommendation: 1. Continue to take 160mg of vometinib orally qd and reexamine after 2 months, including cranial enhanced MRI, chest CT scan, and abdominal and supraclavicular cervical lymph node ultrasound examination. 2. Perform a full set of blood routine and biochemical tests once a month during medication. 3. Desumeb 120mg, subcutaneous injection, once every 4 weeks. 4. Continue antihypertensive, lipid-lowering and other treatments.
Treatment: - 
Western medicine: Desmodium injection 120mg (1.7mL) x 1 bottle, subcutaneous injection, immediately.
Department: Internal Medicine II (Thoracic) Clinic

input:
Admission and discharge records

The patient first developed symptoms on August 8, 2021, and was diagnosed with lung cancer after evaluation. The first treatment (including drug therapy and radiotherapy) was on January 17, 2021. During treatment, we also evaluated the patient's ECOG performance status, and the most recent evaluation date was July 20, 2021.

Historical test results showed that the patient carried HER3 (ERBB3) positive expression, which may affect the choice of treatment and prognosis. In addition, the patient had a history of infectious diseases, including syphilis treponema infection.

Results of genetic testing:

c.2578G>A (p.E860K) of the PALB2 gene is a missense mutation, which is associated with a high risk of breast and pancreatic cancer and is sensitive to PARP inhibitors. The mutation frequency of this gene is 26.56%, indicating that its expression in cells is relatively common.

c.610G>T (p.E204*) of the TP53 gene is a nonsense mutation, which may lead to the loss of function of the TP53 protein, thereby affecting the regulation of the cell cycle. This mutation has been recorded many times in the COSMIC database and has been detected in many tumors such as lung cancer.

The test report pointed out that the frequency of occurrence of the above gene mutations in cells and the biological effects they may cause need to be taken seriously in subsequent treatment and monitoring.

The test was conducted by Wang Shaoling in the laboratory. The report was reviewed on July 28, 2023 and completed on August 1, 2023. json results:

AFFILIATED HOSPITAL OF HEBEI UNIVERSITY
Outpatient medical records

Gender: Male
Clinical department: Oncology Clinic (North)
Clinical time: 2023-10-17 08:37
Date of admission: 2019-12-29

Chief complaint: Left lung adenocarcinoma diagnosed for more than 2 years, first diagnosed on 2020-05-17.
Current medical history: The patient underwent chest CT examination due to chest tightness and left chest pain since July 6, 2021, and multiple nodules and left pleural effusion were found in the fissure area of ​​the left lobe. Bronchoscopy did not reveal obvious tumors. The patient underwent thoracentesis on September 1, 2021, and the pleural effusion pathology was considered to be adenocarcinoma, indicating that there were signs of metastasis of lung adenocarcinoma.
An enhanced MRI of the head was performed in August 2021, and no significant metastasis was found. Whole-body bone imaging showed increased bone salt metabolism in the spine, anterior ribs, and mid-humerus, suggesting the presence of bone metastasis. Bone salt metabolism in the left 8-10 ribs also showed an increase, which may be caused by fractures.

EGFR gene testing performed in an external hospital showed 19del mutations with an abundance of 58.31%. The mutation ratio of TP53 exon5 was 34.76%. Since September 8, 2021, the patient has started taking 125 mg of icotinib three times a day for targeted therapy.
The reexamination on October 22, 2021 showed that the condition was assessed as PD, the lesions in the left lower lobe of the lung were enlarged, and the left pleural effusion increased. Pleural effusion gene testing showed that the abundance of EGFR 19del E746-A750del mutations was 17.52%, and the abundance of missense mutations in TP53 exon 5 was 10.51%.

From November 19, 2021 to April 19, 2022, the patient received bevacizumab treatment for a total of 6 cycles, and the results showed that the disease was stable (SD) and the pleural effusion was reduced. In addition, the patient also received regular injections of denosumab for anti-bone metastasis treatment.
The tumor marker test on April 29, 2022 showed normal, including NSE, CEA, CY211 and SCC indicators. Compared with the previous results, the enhanced CT of the neck, chest and abdomen showed that the range of soft tissue lesions in the left lower lobe of the lung was reduced, and the left pleural effusion was also reduced. No obvious abnormalities were found in the enhanced MRI of the head, and the treatment effect was evaluated as partial remission (PR).

In May 2022, bevacizumab was discontinued due to medical insurance reimbursement issues. The tumor marker test on July 1, 2022 was still normal, and the examination of the neck, chest and abdomen showed stable condition.

Other medical data:
- The patient has a history of infectious diseases hepatitis B and human papillomavirus infection.
- In terms of the nervous system, the patient has a history of depression and cerebral infarction.
- Inflammation of the urinary and reproductive system.
- Eye, ear, nose and throat related, the patient has a history of glaucoma.
- The date of immune detection is January 15, 2021.
- The first symptom appeared on September 25, 2022, and the first imaging diagnosis was on April 29, 2020.
- The specific treatment method for the tumor is targeted therapy.
- The surgical site includes brain surgery due to metastasis.
- The results of genetic marker detection are that both HER2 and HER4 are non-20 insertions, and KEAP1 is positive.

Patient admission record

Basic information of the patient:

Surname: Male, age: 66 years old, admission date: XXXX-XX-XX

Main complaint: Left upper lobe adenocarcinoma more than 5 years after surgery, recurrence and metastasis after chemotherapy, targeted therapy

Summary of medical history:
The patient underwent left upper lobectomy in another hospital on XXXX-XX, and pathology suggested adenocarcinoma. Genetic testing showed EGFR exon 20 insertion mutation. PET/CT examination revealed multiple metastases, including no high metabolic foci at the bronchial stump after left upper lobectomy, new diffuse multiple nodules in both lungs, mild diffuse increase in metabolism in both lungs, which was considered to be multiple metastases in both lungs, new thickening of multiple interlobular septa in the right lung, and new small effusions in the bilateral chest and pericardium. Metastatic lymph nodes were newly found in the 7th area of ​​the mediastinum and the right hilum. After that, the patient began to be enrolled in a clinical trial (TAK788) and was treated with pemetrexed + carboplatin. The patient was reviewed regularly for chest enhanced CT, and then thrombolytic therapy was performed. During this period, the disease progressed, and oral TAK788 was started on XXXX-XX. After taking the medicine, symptoms such as increased blood pressure, oral metoprolol, small reddish rashes on the outer thighs and chest and back, and ALT briefly increased to 71 and then returned to normal after oral liver protection drugs. The patient's condition is currently stable, and he continues to take oral targeted therapy with vometinib.

Examination results: The patient's ALT is 2740, AST is 1440; severe liver function damage. Recent chest CT examinations showed that the metastatic lesions in both lungs were slightly enlarged, and there was no obvious change in pleural effusion. The head MRI showed that the brain metastasis caused the same as before.

Diagnosis: Left upper lobe adenocarcinoma, brain and lung metastasis, bone metastasis, EGFR20+, targeted therapy

Treatment: Continue to take vometinib 160mg qd for 2 months, and recheck chest CT plain scan, abdominal and supraclavicular cervical lymph node color Doppler ultrasound and other examinations. Blood routine and biochemical full set are reviewed every month during medication.

Recommendation: Considering the patient's slow progression of the disease, it is recommended to continue observation and strengthen drug treatment. If further treatment is required, denosumab injection can be considered for treatment. At the same time, attention should be paid to blood pressure and lipid-lowering treatment.

Clinical doctor: Zhao Jun (Internal Medicine II (Chest) Clinic)

Admission and discharge records:

Patient Zhao Jun, medical record number 10312, was admitted to the outpatient department of the Department of Thoracic Oncology, Peking University Cancer Hospital on October 17, 2023. The main complaint was adenocarcinoma of the left upper lobe with brain metastasis for more than 2 years. The current medical history shows that the patient had shortness of breath and coughing up bloody sputum for more than 2 months. Past medical history shows that on November 29, 2022, a CT scan at an external hospital found a mass in the left upper lobe of the lung, accompanied by a little obstructive inflammation. The lesion may surround the left pulmonary artery hepatic trunk and some of its branches. At the same time, there are scattered solid nodules in both lungs, which are considered to be metastatic. There is mild emphysema, slightly larger lymph nodes in the mediastinum and left hilum, and local thickening of the left pleura. On December 9, 2022, the pathological consultation of Peking Union Medical College determined that it was a tumor cell in the left upper lobe of the lung, and squamous cell carcinoma was not excluded. On January 7, 2023, an enhanced MRI of the head at an external hospital showed possible metastasis in the right frontal lobe and left temporal lobe cortex and medulla. On 2023-02-02, the patient was diagnosed with moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma by left lung puncture. No mutation was found in blood gene testing, and PD-L1 (SP263) expression was low. Tissue gene testing showed EGFR21 L858R positive, PIK3CA positive, and MET amplification. The patient started oral Conmena on 2023-03-12, and then started Conmena combined with Cervotinib on 2023-05-21. On 2023-07-19, the external hospital reexamination of chest enhanced CT showed that the left upper lobe tumor was slightly reduced, the small nodules in the anterior segment of the right upper lobe were reduced, the density of the small nodules in the posterior segment of the right upper lobe and the lingular segment of the left upper lobe was reduced, and no effusion was found in the left pleural cavity. On 2023-07-21, the head enhanced MRI showed that the size of the right frontal lobe nodule was 8.9 mm. Subsequently, Conmena combined with Gumetinib was started on 2023-07-21. The most recent examination on 2023-10-12 showed that the tumor from the left hilum to the left upper lobe was generally stable, the small nodules in the anterior segment of the right upper lobe were slightly enlarged, and the nodules in the posterior segment of the right upper lobe were slightly reduced. The patient's current purpose of visiting the doctor is to consult the next treatment plan.

Doctor Zhao Jun, the medical history collected on 2021-04-02 showed that he had papillary adenocarcinoma. The specific gene test results showed that STK11 was positive, RET was rearranged, MET was overexpressed in c-MET, and RB1 was positive. The patient was born on 1966-08-02, the date of admission was 2020-04-28, and the treatment ended on 2021-01-22. json result:

input:
Discharge record: 
The patient, Ms. Meng Yan, 67 years old, was admitted to the hospital for targeted therapy for lung metastasis 16 years after surgery for right lung adenocarcinoma. The patient underwent surgery for right lung adenocarcinoma in another hospital in 2007. The postoperative stage was stage I. Postoperative pathology showed adenocarcinoma, and genetic testing showed EGFR 21 mutation. In December 2014, a small nodule in the left upper lobe was found during a follow-up examination. Genetic testing showed EGFR 21 L858 mutation, and the condition was stable after regular follow-up examinations. In November 2019, the left lung nodule increased in size. In April 2020, a follow-up examination showed that the nodule further increased and fused. It was considered to be a metastatic lesion. Oral Conmena was started. The nodule became smaller and a rash appeared during the follow-up examination. A follow-up examination in September 2020 showed that the lesion had slightly increased in size, and then oral Conmena combined with apatinib was used for treatment. The lesion was smaller after a month of follow-up examination. The medication was continued until January 2022, and the follow-up examination showed that the condition was stable. From January 2022, he started taking osimertinib orally. By February 2023, he had taken osimertinib orally for 1 year. No metastasis was found in the head MRI, and the lung lesions were stable. As of October 2023, the condition was still stable, and it was recommended to continue to consult for follow-up treatment plans.
The patient had a history of hypertension, no history of allergies, and an unknown family history. According to the Hofu TNM staging, he was diagnosed with postoperative targeted treatment of right lung adenocarcinoma (EGFR 21 L858R mutation), lung metastasis and right adrenal metastasis.
Recommendations:
1. For left upper lung progression, apatinib and osimertinib can be considered for oral resection after one month. You can also consult a thoracic surgeon to consider palliative resection of the left lung lesion.
2. For local lesions in the right adrenal gland, SBRT treatment can be considered.
In terms of medical data, the patient also has respiratory diseases such as pulmonary edema, eye, ear, nose and throat related diseases such as conjunctivitis and eye stones, and infectious diseases such as tuberculosis. The name of the disease may be other pathological types of non-small cell lung cancer, with FGFR2 point mutations. The admission date is December 13, 2020. JSON result:

input:
Peking University Cancer Hospital
Beijing Cancer Hospital
Medical Record
Number: T002269842
Date: 2023-10-12
Department: Outpatient Department of Thoracic Oncology
Medical Record Number: -

Chief complaint: Left lung adenocarcinoma for more than 4 years and undergoing multi-stage treatment

Current medical history: The patient was diagnosed with left non-small cell lung adenocarcinoma four years ago with EGFR21 mutation. Initially received first-line treatment with oral Tarceva, but developed resistance after about ten months. Second-line treatment was used from April to December 2020, including pemetrexed, carboplatin and bevacizumab. In January 2021, the disease worsened, and then received third-line treatment with paclitaxel and bevacizumab, and started to use Conmana in combination on May 20, 2021. Then used fourth-line treatment with vometinib until January 2023, when vometinib was combined with anlotinib. In May 2023, the disease progressed again and was treated with ametinib, and intrapleural infusion of cisplatin and endorphin was performed. On June 1, 2023, pembrolizumab combined with vinorelbine was started for 2 cycles, and ametinib was taken orally at the same time. Basic information of the patient: The patient is a male, aged 60 years, and was admitted for immunotherapy combined with targeted therapy for pleural mesothelioma.

Review of medical history: The patient has been diagnosed with pleural mesothelioma since 2020 and has undergone a series of treatments. The pathological report showed that the left lung puncture biopsy confirmed the infiltrative growth of tumor cells, which was consistent with pleural mesothelioma. PET-CT examination showed significant thickening of the left pleura and peritoneum, accompanied by increased FDG uptake. In the past few months, the patient's condition has progressed, with nodular thickening of the left pleura and peritoneum, left pleural effusion and compressive atelectasis of the left lower lung.

Gene test results showed that ALK was positive and ROS1 had G3032R rearrangement. In addition, the patient also had other diseases such as severe arrhythmia, pulmonary bullae, gastrointestinal fistula, hyperuricemia, etc.

Diagnosis: The patient was diagnosed with pleural mesothelioma immunotherapy and targeted therapy, accompanied by pleural effusion, atelectasis, pain and other symptoms of pleural mesothelioma.

Recommendation: In view of the patient's condition, it is recommended to continue albumin paclitaxel chemotherapy, and cell therapy can be selected later.

instruction: Your task is to extract medical information from the input report and output it in JSON format, and output NA for information not mentioned in the report
input:
Discharge record:
The patient was admitted to the hospital for stage IB postoperative left lung adenocarcinoma, secondary renal malignant tumor (adenocarcinoma), and brain metastasis. Physical examination at admission revealed multiple new solid nodules in the left lung, some of which were the same as before, and some of which shrank and disappeared. The largest one was about 0.5-0.3cm in size, and metastasis was to be excluded; there was no sign of metastasis in the brain. After radiotherapy, radiotherapy ended 4 months ago, and no targeted therapy was performed. During follow-up, multiple micro- and small nodules in both lungs were found without high metabolism, and follow-up observation was recommended. In addition, multiple rib fractures were partially accompanied by high metabolism, which required further clinical evaluation. The patient's rib lesions were not considered to be metastatic for the time being, and the left lung lesions were not considered to be recurrent. After multidisciplinary consultation, the left lung lesions could not be punctured to obtain specimens. Recent reexaminations showed that the scattered small nodules in both lungs were the same as before, and new metastases appeared in the brain. Therefore, it is necessary to continue to improve genetic testing, especially PD-L1 and STK11. The current treatment plan is mainly medical drug treatment, and SRS for intracranial metastases and SBRT for renal metastases are considered.

Medical data insertion: 
The patient's discharge date is 2023-09-27, and the brain metastasis site is brain stem metastasis. The first symptom time is 2021-11-06, the pathological diagnosis date is 2021-05-12, the date of birth is 1983-04-18, and the STK11 test result is positive. JSON result:

instruction: Your task is to extract medical information from the input report and output it in JSON format, and output NA for information not mentioned in the report
input: Dr. Zhao Jun treated a patient with right lung small cell lung cancer in the outpatient clinic of the Department of Thoracic Oncology on November 9, 2023. According to the medical records from July 3, 2023 to September 19, 2023, the patient had a right hilar mass and possible pulmonary CA. After treatment with carboplatin + VP16 + slulizumab, the patient's condition improved, the right hilar mass disappeared, and the enlarged lymph nodes in the mediastinum and right hilar shrank. In addition, the patient still had the right upper lung and left hilar tumors that needed to be differentiated from inflammatory lymph nodes, and there were no signs of abnormal metabolism in the right pleura and brain. It was confirmed as small cell carcinoma in the pathology report on July 13, 2023. Comprehensive medical data showed that the patient was first diagnosed with mixed invasive mucinous and non-mucinous adenocarcinoma on October 24, 2019, and there were MET amplification and MET fusion, etc., and brain metastasis surgery was performed on October 29, 2021. The patient's condition is currently stable and we recommend continued close follow-up and treatment. json result:

Main complaint: Late stage of left upper lobe adenocarcinoma
Medical history: On 2020-12-28, it was first confirmed as left upper bronchial adenocarcinoma by bronchoscopic pathology. No clear cancer cells were found in the left upper bronchial brushings. Bronchial lavage fluid showed mild atypical glandular cells.
The PET-CT performed at Bethune Hospital on 2021-12-27 showed a high-metabolism soft tissue mass near the apex of the left upper lobe and the oblique fissure pleura, which was considered to be peripheral lung cancer. At the same time, multiple nodular shadows were observed in the bilateral pleura and the left pleura, and some nodules were metabolically active, suggesting possible metastasis. Multiple high-metabolism lymph nodes in the left hilum, mediastinum, and right clavicle were all considered metastases. Brain MRI did not find metastasis.
Genetic testing results showed EGFR19 deletion mutation. Oral osimertinib treatment was started in January 2022. Right cervical lymph node puncture on January 11, 2022 showed fibrous fat and a small amount of muscle tissue. Chest enhanced CT on February 17, 2022 showed changes in the left upper lung after treatment, with a size of about 23*15mm. Multiple enlarged lymph nodes with a diameter of no more than 9mm were observed in the supraclavicular region and axilla on both sides.

Diagnosis: Advanced microinvasive adenocarcinoma of the left upper lobe, HER4 (ERBB4) positive, KRAS G12C mutation, liver metastasis, meningeal and brainstem metastasis.
Treatment recommendations:
1. Continue to take osimertinib 80mg orally once a day and review regularly.

3. Strengthen monitoring of liver function, and use coenzyme Q10 and Yishanfu liver protection treatment to reduce drug-induced liver injury.
4. Recheck chest enhanced CT and abdominal and cervical lymph node ultrasound after 1 month, and check blood routine and liver and kidney function.

Disposition:
1. Follow up and perform regular chest, abdominal and neck examinations.
2. Pay attention to monitoring and treating side effects such as diarrhea caused by osimertinib.

Past history: Denies smoking history. No history of hypertension, diabetes and coronary heart disease.

Discharge record:
Patient Zhao Jun visited the outpatient department of chest tumors in Peking University Cancer Hospital on October 26, 2023. The main symptoms were occasional cough, fatigue, shortness of breath, and right chest pain. According to the results of chest CT examination in an external hospital, there were multiple solid nodules and ground glass nodules in both lungs, a small amount of effusion in the right pleural cavity, and slightly enlarged mediastinal lymph nodes. The outpatient diagnosis was sigmoid colon cancer, hypertension (with cough) and adenocarcinoma of the right lower lobe of the lung, and postoperative (adenocarcinoma, stage IIIA).

According to the medical data on July 19, 2021, the patient had ALK gene fusion, EGFR gene mutation was G719C type, HER2 (ERBB2) gene had non-20 insertion and 20 insertion mutations, and RET gene was deleted. The patient's past history included hypertension for 10 years, depression, oral Norvasc and venlafaxine. At present, compound licorice tablets are continued to be taken orally, and atelizumab treatment is continued at a dose of 1200 mg, once every three weeks, and reviewed every three months. The postoperative adjuvant treatment plan is one year. Family history and physical examination showed no abnormalities. Dr. Zhao Jun was responsible for the patient's treatment. JSON results:

Report:

Patient information:

The patient's name is Meng Yan, female, 67 years old. The main complaint is right lung adenocarcinoma 16 years after surgery, lung metastasis targeted treatment.

Medical history:

The patient underwent right lung adenocarcinoma surgery in 2007, postoperative pathology was adenocarcinoma, and the genetic test result was EGFR 21 mutation. A small nodule was found in the left upper lobe during a chest CT examination in December 2014, and the genetic test result was EGFR 21 L858 mutation. Regular follow-up was stable thereafter. However, during the follow-up in April 2020, it was found that the left lung nodule was enlarged, and metastasis was considered, and oral Conmana was started. One month later, the left lung nodule was smaller and the rash was obvious. However, oral Conmana combined with apatinib treatment was started in February 2021, and the lesion was smaller after a review one month later. At present, the lung lesions are stable and the right adrenal lesions are progressing.

Medical data:

According to the data provided, the malignant tumor is non-Hodgkin's lymphoma; the pathological date is 2021-07-29; RET fusion; the first diagnosis date of the disease is 2021-09-02; the date of brain metastasis is 2021-03-21; the endocrine and immune system diseases are diabetes; the nervous system diseases are spinal cord compression; the digestive system diseases are tuberculosis; MET overexpression; RB1 positive; the specific treatment methods for tumors are anti-vascular targeted therapy and endocrine therapy, etc.

Recommendations:

Based on the patient's condition, the following recommendations are made:

1. If the patient's left upper lung lesion progresses, apatinib and osimertinib oral treatment can be considered for review after one month. A thoracic surgeon can be consulted to consider palliative resection of the left lung lesion.

2. SBRT treatment can be considered for local lesions in the right adrenal gland.

Note:

Please formulate the best treatment plan based on the patient's specific situation and fully communicate with the patient. The above recommendations are for reference only. json results:

Discharge record: 
The patient is a 34-year-old female, self-funded patient. At the time of admission, the patient's main complaint was wedge resection of right upper lobe adenocarcinoma. The medical history showed that the patient had a nodule in the right upper lobe on November 3, 2021. The puncture biopsy results showed right lung adenocarcinoma, no sensitive mutation genes were found, and PD-L1 expression was 49%. The patient underwent preoperative neoadjuvant treatment from November 2021 to January 27, 2022, including 4 cycles of pemetrexed + carboplatin + K drug, followed by a single drug K drug. On March 15, 2022, one cycle of pemetrexed + K drug treatment was completed, and then in April 2022, a wedge resection of the right upper lobe was performed at West China Hospital. Postoperative pathology showed invasive non-mucinous adenocarcinoma with intravascular cancer thrombus and tumor involvement of the visceral pleura. Subsequently, during a follow-up examination at an external hospital, it was found that the lung lesions had enlarged, new brain metastases in the right temporal lobe, and enlarged adrenal nodules. The patient underwent genetic testing on June 3, 2022, and the results showed that KEAP1 was positive, PD-L1 was 1%, and MET was fused. After starting oral sirolimus treatment, the brain lesions disappeared, but on August 31, 2023, the right temporal lobe brain lesions recurred, and CEA slowly increased. The patient had a history of thyroid cancer surgery in the past. There was no abnormality in the family history. The final diagnosis was right upper lobe adenocarcinoma after wedge resection, MET fusion, and brain metastasis. It is recommended to consider multifocal progression, perform pleural biopsy puncture again, perform tissue genetic testing, or participate in the TILS study, or take oral anlotinib. The discharge date is January 26, 2023.

Admission and discharge records:

Patient X was reexamined 1 year and 4 months after the end of radiotherapy on 2021-11-02, and hemoptysis occurred in the past week. Routine blood tests showed that the ratio of large platelets was 12.80%, the hematocrit was 52.00%, the hemoglobin was 175.00g/L, and the total number of red blood cells was 5.77*10^12/L. The results of biochemical and tumor marker tests showed that neuron-specific enolase was 16.92ng/ml, apolipoprotein B was 1.09g/L, and uric acid was 464μmol/L. Chest CT showed that the nodules near the hilum of the left upper lobe were the same as before, and the multiple enlarged lymph nodes in the mediastinum, bilateral hilum, and right supraclavicular were the same as before. Patchy and ground-glass shadows appeared beside the mediastinum of the left lung, which was related to changes after radiotherapy. Mild interstitial lesions in the lower lobes of both lungs were the same as before, accompanied by bilateral emphysema. There was no significant change in the pericardial effusion. Abdominal CT showed multiple small cysts in the right kidney as before. No signs of metastasis were found in the head MRI. In addition, the patient rechecked tumor markers on 2021-11-11, and cancer antigen 125 increased to 44.0 (U/ml), and neuron-specific enolase decreased slightly to 16.30 (ng/ml). The abdominal CT results showed that the liver was normal in shape and size, with no abnormal density shadows or abnormal enhancement foci in the parenchyma. Multiple solid low-density nodules in the right kidney were the same as before, and new high-density nodules were found in the gallbladder. The results of neck ultrasound showed multiple lymph nodes in both cervical roots and occlusion of the right internal jugular vein.

Gender: Female
Age: 59 years old
Main complaint: diagnosed with lung cancer 3 years ago, recently found abdominal mass for 3 months
Summary of medical records: The patient went to the First Affiliated Hospital of Dalian Medical University for hemoptysis in September 2020. CT showed high-density nodules in the middle bronchus of the right lung, mass-like shadows in the right lower hilar area, accompanied by obstructive inflammation in the right lower lobe and multiple enlarged lymph nodes in the mediastinum, which were considered to be malignant. On October 27, 2020, a bronchoscopy was performed in Shenyang, and the possibility of small cell lung cancer was high. After that, 7 rounds of chemotherapy and Chinese medicine treatment were received, and the lesions were reduced. Regular follow-up examinations were performed. In June 2023, a mass was found in the right lower abdomen. On July 22, 2023, a CT scan was performed at the Second Hospital of Dalian Medical University, indicating thickening of the ascending colon wall and a mass next to the ascending colon in the right abdominal cavity, which may be lymphoma. Metastatic tumors cannot be ruled out. The lymph nodes in the mesenteric region are enlarged, and there is pelvic effusion. A puncture biopsy of the abdominal lymph nodes was performed. The cytological performance tended to be small cell carcinoma metastasis. After supplementary immunohistochemistry, it was diagnosed as small cell neuroendocrine carcinoma. On September 12, 2023, a reexamination of the abdominal CT revealed a mass in the right lower abdomen, with a maximum cross-sectional area of ​​80*58mm. The boundary with the adjacent ileocecal part and part of the small intestine was unclear, involving the right ureter, which may be a malignant tumor of intestinal origin. The right renal pelvis and ureter were dilated and hydronephrosis, accessory spleen was present, and pelvic effusion was present. On September 24, 2023, a laparotomy was performed in the local hospital. Cancer cells were found in the abdominal effusion, and the lesion could not be removed.

Genetic testing showed FGFR2 rearrangement on December 22, 2021. The first symptoms of brain metastasis appeared on April 20, 2022, in the cerebral hemisphere, which belongs to the category of brain metastasis.

Diagnosis: Peritoneal metastasis of small cell lung cancer after chemotherapy
Recommendation: After the wound recovers, start EC chemotherapy, and perform thyroid function, amylase, lipase, myocardial enzyme, blood routine and biochemical examinations. If there are no contraindications, consider immunotherapy with slulizumab. JSON results:

Main complaint: Advanced lung adenocarcinoma for more than half a year, HER-2 20 insertion mutation

Current medical history: Lung adenocarcinoma has been diagnosed since August 7, 2020. Lung adenocarcinoma was first diagnosed by puncture on May 26, 2019. The patient had c-MET amplification, STK11 positive, FGFR point mutation, EGFR positive, RET positive, and BRCA1 positive. On April 3, 2021, pathological examination was performed again to confirm the above gene status. During the treatment, the first symptoms appeared on April 22, 2021. On August 10, 2021, the first lung surgery was performed. Carboplatin combined with pemetrexed and bevacizumab was used for 4 cycles of treatment, but the treatment was terminated on December 2, 2022.

In May 2023, the patient was enrolled in an open, first-in-human study evaluating BAY 2927088 in subjects with advanced non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) with EGFR and/or HER2 mutations. In the late stage of treatment, the patient developed liver damage, but the condition was relieved to a certain extent. However, due to drug side effects, treatment was suspended from August to September 2023.

2023-08-07 Bone scan results showed: no obvious signs of bone metastasis were found in whole-body planar bone imaging; symmetrical bone salt metabolism active lesions in the long bones of the limbs were reduced compared with before, and pulmonary bone disease was still possible.

2023-08-24 Re-examination of abdominal and pelvic enhanced CT showed: the small cyst in the right lobe of the liver partially disappeared. The multiple small lymph nodes above the diaphragm, in the hepatic hilar area and beside the abdominal aorta were similar to the previous ones.

2023-08-25 Re-examination of chest enhanced CT showed: the tumor in the left upper lobe of the lung was larger than before, the pleura/pericardium was more severely affected, and the pericardial effusion increased. Some of the multiple small nodules in both lungs increased, while others remained unchanged. Some of the multiple metastatic lymph nodes in the supraclavicular, bilateral hilar, and mediastinal regions increased.

On August 28, 2023, the enhanced CT of the neck lymph nodes showed that the multiple lymph nodes in the upper mediastinum and bilateral supraclavicular regions were slightly larger than before.

On August 31 and September 21, 2023, the patient received 2 cycles of 300 mg of trastuzumab.

On October 9, 2023, the CT scan showed that compared with August 25, 2023, the cancer mass in the left upper lobe of the lung was reduced, the involvement of the pleura/pericardium was reduced, and the pericardial effusion was reduced. Some of the multiple small nodules in both lungs remained unchanged, and some of the multiple metastatic lymph nodes in the supraclavicular, bilateral hilar, and mediastinal regions were reduced.

PR was evaluated on October 10, 2023.

Past history: The patient denied a history of smoking, hypertension, diabetes, and coronary heart disease. Family history was denied. Urogenital system diseases included hypertensive nephropathy and uremia.

Medical record number: 841450
Date: 2023-11-02
Department: Outpatient Department of Thoracic Oncology

Main complaint: Diagnosed with thymic squamous cell carcinoma for 6 months.
Current medical history: Chest CT scan at an external hospital showed: a mass appeared in the anterior mediastinum, which was closely connected to the anterior mediastinal blood vessels.

PET/CT scan results on 2017-10-19 showed:
1. A soft tissue density mass appeared in the anterior mediastinum, accompanied by high metabolic activity, which may be a malignant change, suspected of malignant thymic tumor. The boundary with the main mediastinal blood vessels, pericardium and left pleura was not obvious. Multiple metastatic nodules appeared in the bilateral pleura and interlobar pleura. A small amount of fluid accumulated in the pericardium. Fluid accumulated in the bilateral pleural cavity, which was more serious on the left side. Part of the left lower lung was not fully expanded.
2. No obvious abnormal metabolism was found in the brain. Calcification of the left semioval center. Inflammation or nonspecific absorption was shown in the oropharynx and anus. Inflammation of the left maxillary sinus. The nasal septum was shifted to the right. Left lower lung bronchiectasis with inflammation. Fatty liver and left kidney cysts are present.

2017-11-9 CT scan results:
An irregular mass of about 63×35mm in size can be seen in the left anterior superior mediastinum, showing uneven enhancement and involving the mediastinal pleura. Multiple small nodules can be seen in both lungs, the largest of which is about 8mm. No obvious enlarged lymph nodes were found in the mediastinum, bilateral hilar, supraclavicular region and bilateral axillae.

2017-10-26 puncture biopsy confirmed thymic squamous cell carcinoma.
2017-11-10 TP chemotherapy was started, and the effect was evaluated as partial remission (PR) after 2 cycles. Currently, 4 cycles of treatment have been completed, and the reexamination results have not yet been obtained. The last chemotherapy ended on 2018-03-07.

2018-1-25 Reexamination CT showed: The thymoma continued to shrink and is currently about 17x29mm in size.

On March 15, 2018, he received radiotherapy with a dose of 95% PTV50.4Gy and 95% P-GTV64Gy.
On March 17, 2018, local chest CT showed that the thymic tumor had shrunk compared with before.
On May 4, 2018, radiotherapy ended.
On December 12, 2018, chest CT reexamination showed that the tumor had not increased.
On March 13, 2019, chest CT reexamination showed that the lesions in the mediastinum had not increased significantly.
On March 14, 2019, abdominal MRI test: No. 944950, the liver morphology and size were normal, and long T1 and long T2 signal nodules appeared in the S2 and S4 regions of the liver, the largest of which was about 14x11mm.

Medical data supplement:
On October 17, 2020, he received treatment for the first time.
On January 27, 2021, a detailed medical history was collected.
On 2021-08-21, an immune test was performed, and the results showed that HER2 (ERBB2) was non-20 insertion, KRAS mutation was G13D, and MET had MET14 insertion. The patient also suffered from severe renal insufficiency and gastrointestinal stones. In addition, the patient also had endocrine and immune system diseases, including psoriasis and Guillain-Barré syndrome.

Doctor: Zhao Jun
