Existing studies have shown that patients with positive MRD (molecular residual disease) in peripheral blood after treatment have a poor prognosis. This research result is of great significance in lung cancer patients.

Submitted by: Beijing Cancer Hospital
Submitted project: OncoMRD-Lung-B
Retained lesions)
Statistics of 32 patients with first-node plasma samples within 4 months after treatment showed a ctDNA detection rate of 53% (54%), a 36-month free progression rate (FFP) of 0% in the ctDNA-positive group, and a 36-month free progression rate (FFP) of 93% in the ctDNA-negative group, with a hazard ratio of 43.4. Therefore, ctDNA testing can stably identify MRD in localized lung cancer, thus providing the possibility of personalized adjuvant therapy during the period when the tumor burden is very small.

Medication history:
2022-06 to 2022-08, carboplatin + pemetrexed
2023-03, atezolizumab
In a clinical study (TRACERx) for early non-small cell lung cancer, a total of 24 patients were included in the study of the recurrence risk prediction value of postoperative ctDNA detection, of which the recurrence rate of the ctDNA-positive group was 93% (13/14) and the recurrence rate of the ctDNA-negative group was 10% (1/10). Dynamic detection of ctDNA can analyze the subclonal evolution of recurrent NSCLC, evaluate the response to adjuvant therapy, and provide guidance for personalized treatment before clinical recurrence.

Response, provide guidance for personalized treatment before clinical recurrence (PMID: 28445469). In a retrospective study of patients with locally advanced non-small cell lung cancer, follow-up plasma ctDNA was analyzed in 65 patients who received radical chemoradiotherapy, of which 28 patients received consolidation immune checkpoint inhibition (ICI) therapy. The study showed that patients with no ctDNA detected after radical chemoradiotherapy had a better prognosis regardless of whether they received ICI consolidation therapy, and there was no significant difference in the progression-free rate. Patients with ctDNA detected who received ICI consolidation therapy had a better prognosis than those who did not receive ICI consolidation therapy.

2023-07-02 Head enhanced MRI reexamination showed: multiple metastatic lesions in the brain were partially unchanged, partially enlarged, and newly occurred.

2023-07-08 Started taking Tagrisso, and intermittent convulsions occurred after taking the medicine.
2023-07-18 Started receiving gamma knife brain radiotherapy.

2023-09-18 Head enhanced MRI reexamination also showed similar changes. The left parotid nodule was the same as before and needs further investigation. The left mastoiditis condition remained unchanged.
2023-09-22 The chief physician suggested the use of suvotinib and bevacizumab to control brain edema. If the treatment effect is not good, chemotherapy should be considered. The patient refused to use suvotinib and hoped to try chemotherapy.
2023-09-22 One cycle of bevacizumab treatment was performed at Jingxi Cancer Hospital.
2023-09-26 The chest enhanced CT of our hospital showed that the tumor in the upper lobe of the left lung was slightly reduced. Multiple metastases in both lungs and the left pleura were slightly enlarged.
2023-09-26 Our hospital performed one cycle of pemetrexed treatment.
2023-09-28 Jingxi Cancer Hospital performed a puncture biopsy of the left lower lung mass and found a large amount of necrosis and small foci of small cell lung carcinoma. Immunohistochemistry did not support squamous cell carcinoma, showing partial expression of neuroendocrine markers and partial expression of TTF1, tending to atypical carcinoid. FLSH test did not find MET gene amplification.
2023-10-19 The Department of Thoracic Medicine of our hospital performed C1 cycle of bevacizumab and irinotecan treatment.
2023-10-21 Our hospital reviewed the chest enhanced CT compared with the chest CT on 2023-09-24. The tumor status of the left upper lobe did not change, and the multiple metastases of the left lung and left pleura were slightly enlarged.

Admission record: Basic information
The patient is 73 years old and has a 40-year history of smoking, but has quit smoking for 5 years. Recently diagnosed with squamous cell lung cancer, the condition showed cT3N1M1a, belonging to stage IVA. A bronchoscopy was performed on 23.10.10, and pulmonary fibrosis, emphysema, and bullae were found. The patient also suffers from coronary heart disease and has undergone 2 stent implantations in 18 years, as well as chronic atrophic gastritis, gastric ulcers and other diseases. Usually take aspirin, atorvastatin, bisoprolol fumarate, amlodipine besylate and other drugs for treatment.

Medical data insertion part:
According to medical data, the patient had the first imaging diagnosis on April 23, 2020, and was diagnosed with fetal adenocarcinoma. The patient began treatment on October 4, 2021, including white purple + cisplatin chemotherapy. The patient also suffers from digestive system diseases such as fatty liver and obstruction. The first lung resection was performed on May 5, 2021. The patient's medical history was collected on November 16, 2021, and the date of admission was February 17, 2020.

In addition to squamous cell lung carcinoma, the patient was diagnosed with ROS1 rearrangement, NTRK rearrangement, MET amplification, and ALK translocation and rearrangement. The patient was first diagnosed with fetal adenocarcinoma on August 3, 2020. The patient underwent immunoassay testing on January 31, 2021.

Specific tumor treatments include stem cell transplantation and particle implantation. In addition, the patient also had brain metastases to the thalamus. json results:

Date: 2023-02-23

The patient complained that he had been diagnosed with squamous cell lung cancer for two years. In the current medical history, the patient underwent surgery for stage IB left upper lobe adenosquamous carcinoma in March 2020. After surgery, he received six cycles of chemotherapy with white purple + loboplatin, and then rested. In April 2021, due to enlarged mediastinal lymph nodes, he underwent six cycles of chemotherapy with carboplatin combined with pemetrexed, followed by mediastinal radiotherapy at a local hospital. The genetic test result was EGFR20 insertion mutation.

Recent chest CT showed that after surgery for the left upper lobe, a small amount of pleural effusion on the left side increased compared with before, localized incomplete expansion of both lungs was the same as before, and scattered small nodules in both lungs were the same as before. Bilateral partial rib fractures were the same as before. Head MR showed: 1. New enhanced nodules were seen in the left cerebellar hemisphere, tending to metastasize; 2. A few ischemic foci in the brain were roughly the same as before. Abdominal CT and neck color Doppler ultrasound showed no obvious signs of metastasis.

The diagnosis upon admission was squamous cell lung carcinoma (postoperative, left upper lobe adenosquamous carcinoma, bilateral lung metastasis?), bone metastasis (to be investigated). The patient received specific tumor treatments such as cell therapy.

Record completed.

The brain metastasis site is brainstem metastasis; the discharge date is 2022-02-20; the first imaging diagnosis time is November 11, 2020; the neurological disease is Parkinson's; the record date is 2021-03-20; the genetic test result is EGFR20 insertion mutation; the specific tumor treatment method is cell therapy; the admission date is 2020-11-8.

instruction: Your task is to extract medical information from the input report and output it in JSON format, and output NA for information not mentioned in the report
input:
Patient Xiaoyun Shangzhi has been diagnosed with squamous cell lung cancer for 2 years. On March 2, 2020, he underwent surgery for adenosquamous carcinoma of the left upper lobe of the lung and was in stage IB. He then underwent 6 cycles of chemotherapy with carboplatin and lobaplatin, and then rested. In April 2021, due to enlarged mediastinal lymph nodes, he underwent 6 cycles of chemotherapy with carboplatin combined with pemetrexed, and then received mediastinal radiotherapy at a local hospital. Genetic testing showed EGFR20 insertion mutation. On January 11, 2022, multiple new solid nodules were found in both lungs, of which the larger ones had large and small metastases to be excluded, and there were no signs of metastasis in the brain. After hormone treatment, chest enhanced CT showed that some small nodules shrank and disappeared, metastases to be excluded, and the left pleural effusion decreased.

MDT recommended a re-examination of chest CT, which needs to be combined with preoperative CT comparison, and the possibility of a second primary is not ruled out. Hormone treatment is currently continuing and gradually reduced. Radiotherapy is expected to end in April 2022, and no targeted therapy has been performed. Recently, our hospital's PET-CT showed that there was no high metabolism in the postoperative bronchial stump of the left upper lobe, and no high metabolism in multiple small nodules in both lungs. Follow-up is recommended. In addition, multiple rib fractures were found, some of which were accompanied by high metabolism. Please consult in combination with clinical conditions. The ribs are not considered to be metastatic for the time being, and the left lung lesions are not considered to be recurrent. Radiotherapy is expected to end in July. No abnormalities were found in cranial MRI and color ultrasound, and no abnormalities were found in chest and abdominal CT.

The most recent review showed that there was no tumor recurrence in chest, abdominal CT, brain MRI, and supraclavicular B-ultrasound. Genetic testing results showed EGFR20 insertion mutation in tissue specimens, and PDL1 expression was 90%. The outpatient diagnosis was stage IB postoperative left lung adenocarcinoma, secondary renal malignant tumor, and brain metastasis. The treatment plan is mainly medical drug treatment, considering elective SRS for intracranial metastases, and SBRT for renal metastases if necessary. json results:

Discharge record: 
The patient visited the outpatient department of the Department of Radiotherapy, Peking University Cancer Hospital on October 24, 2023. The patient still has intermittent chest pain, but his condition is basically stable, with no other obvious discomfort. The patient's myocardial enzymes, amylase, and glycosylated hemoglobin were reexamined on October 21, 2020. There was no abnormality. Thyroid function T3 and T4 were slightly elevated, and blood routine and liver and kidney function were normal. On October 29, 2020, a lung CT scan was performed due to cough, which showed interstitial inflammation in the left lung, accompanied by cough, and treatment-related interstitial pneumonia was considered. Medical data showed that the patient had uveitis. On November 10, 2020, oral hormone prednisone tablets were started for treatment. Currently, 8 tablets have been taken orally, and CT showed that the inflammation in the lungs was significantly absorbed. On November 19, 2020, oral dexamethasone treatment was started, and the condition was still good. External hospital examinations showed normal liver and kidney function. On December 15, 2020, after stopping dexamethasone, there was occasional cough, and no metastasis was found in the head MRI and abdominal CT. ECT examination on 2020-12-16 showed that some bone salt metabolism was slightly reduced, which was considered to be a benign lesion. Medical data showed HER2 (ERBB2) insertion. 2020-12-21 CT of our hospital showed that the nodules near the hilum of the left upper lobe were the same as before, and the multiple enlarged lymph nodes in the mediastinum, bilateral hilum, and right supraclavicular region were similar to the previous ones. There were patchy and ground-glass shadows with an increased range beside the left mediastinum, and new lesions beside the right mediastinum. Considered to be changes after radiotherapy, further investigation is required. Medical data showed that RB1 was positive, RET was missing, fused, and FGFR1 was abnormal. No metastasis was found in the bone ECT reexamination on 2020-12-22, cytokeratin was slightly increased, and the efficacy was evaluated as PR. The reexamination results on 2021-4-6 showed that indicators such as blood routine and tumor markers were within the normal range, indicating that the patient's condition was stable. Overall, the patient's condition has improved during the treatment, but still needs close attention. json results:

1. Admission and discharge records:

Date: 2023-10-23-
Medical record number: 19272
Department: Outpatient Department of Thoracic Oncology.

Main complaint: Continuous cough and chest pain for nearly a month.

Current medical history: The patient underwent chest CT on 2023-10-01, which showed no nodules in the lungs, but the report did not show any. On 2023-10-08, an external hospital chest enhanced CT showed a dense shadow in the right upper lobe of the lung, about 1.7*1.6cm in size, which was considered malignant. Bilateral lung cancer lymphangitis. Multiple enlarged lymph nodes in the bilateral clavicular region and mediastinum. Bilateral pleural thickening and pleural effusion. On 2023-10-11, an external hospital head enhanced MEI showed multiple abnormal strong nodules in the brain, which was considered metastasis. On 2023-10-13, an external hospital bone scan showed multiple bone metastases in the bilateral ribs, multiple vertebrae of the spine, and sacrum. On 2023-10-13, a supraclavicular lymph node puncture biopsy at an external hospital showed consistent with pulmonary mucoepidermoid carcinoma (intermediate grade), with cancer thrombi found in the blood vessels.

Medical data: {'Pathology date': '2021-01-03', 'HER3 (ERBB3)': ['Positive'], 'First pathological diagnosis time (puncture, postoperative pathology, etc.)': '2020-03-17', 'Date of birth': '1970-03-27', 'ECOG date': '2021-04-27', 'RET': ['Point mutation'], 'BRCA': ['Positive'], 'BRAF': ['Positive', 'V600E'], 'STK11': ['Positive'], 'Pathology type': ['Adenocarcinoma in situ'], 'KRAS': ['G12A', 'G13D'], 'NTRK': ['NTRK1', 'Fusion']}

The patient developed chest pain on 2023-10-19 and took oxycodone sustained-release tablets 10mgQ12h orally. Paroxysmal cough, occasionally with blood in sputum, now taking Aju tablets orally. Poor appetite, less food intake, weight loss of 15kg in the past 2 months.

Past history: The patient has a history of smoking for more than 20 years, 20 cigarettes a day, and quit smoking in 2019. In July 2023, there was a thrombosis in the right lower limb, and the symptoms improved after anticoagulant treatment. On August 6, 2023, a cerebral infarction occurred, and conservative treatment was performed. Now there is weakened muscle strength in the right upper limb and left lower limb. On August 24, 2023, a pulmonary embolism occurred, anticoagulant treatment was received and a filter was placed, and now rivaroxaban and aspirin are taken orally. He has a history of hypertension for 3 years and is poorly controlled. He denies a history of diabetes and coronary heart disease. He denies a history of allergies. He denies a history of hepatitis or tuberculosis.

Family history: Denied family history of tumor.

Physical examination: The patient's ECOG score was 1.

Outpatient diagnosis: Lung cancer with lymph node metastasis. JSON result:

Western Beijing Cancer Hospital

Date of visit: 2023-10-13
Gender: Male
Age: 49 years old
Record date: 2020-11-25
Chief complaint: Progression of right lung adenocarcinoma after multiple rounds of treatment, targeted therapy.
Medical history: The patient had right middle lobe adenocarcinoma with bone and brain metastases, EGFR21 L861Q mutation, and had been taking afatinib since September 10, 2020. Multiple reexaminations showed that the lung and brain lesions had been alleviated. On December 8, 2020, cranial MRI showed multiple metastases in the bilateral cerebrum and right cerebellum, and some lesions were slightly enlarged. Chest CT showed that the lesions in the right middle lobe of the lung were stable, and the right hilar lymph nodes were slightly smaller than before. On December 27, 2020, the MRI of the head showed that the condition was basically similar to the previous one; the local enhanced nodules on the skin and subcutaneous part of the head were slightly more obvious than before. The third cycle of chemotherapy began on June 7, 2021, using bevacizumab, pemetrexed and carboplatin. From March 6, 2021, the patient switched to oral osimertinib. On March 29, 2021, the enhanced MRI of the brain showed that the condition of the scalp and subcutaneous nodules was basically maintained, but the multiple metastases in the bilateral brain and right cerebellum were larger than before. On April 28, 2021, radiotherapy was started at Beijing Swelling Hospital, involving brain metastases. On May 9, 2021, the second cycle of bevacizumab + pemetrexed + carboplatin chemotherapy was performed. The results of the chest CT scan on June 1, 2021 showed that the right middle lobe of the lung had a mass with distal obstructive changes similar to the previous time, and the condition of multiple metastatic lymph nodes in the right hilum and left supraclavicular part of the mediastinum remained unchanged. On July 14, 2021, chest CT showed that the right middle lobe mass with distal obstructive changes was roughly the same as before. Bevacizumab treatment was switched to on July 15, 2021, and on September 27, 2021, it was changed to afatinib 40mg once a day. On December 16, 2021, afatinib is still being taken orally, and cranial MRI showed that the abnormal signal of the right cerebellum was enhanced.

Past history: The patient has a history of hypertension for more than 4 years and is currently receiving nifedipine treatment.
Allergy history: None.
Family history: Denies cancer or other major genetic diseases.
Diagnosis: Targeted treatment of right lung adenocarcinoma EGFR21 L861Q, cerebellar metastasis, bone metastasis, targeted therapy, hypertension, and difficulty in movement and pain after radiotherapy for secondary brain malignant tumors.
Recommendations: 1. Outpatient evaluation shows that the disease has progressed compared with the previous one, and it is recommended to take suvotinib 300mg orally once a day.
2. Regular check-ups, including chest CT, cranial MRI, and abdominal and cervical lymph node ultrasound.

- HER4 (ERBB4): Positive

- First symptom time: 2021-06-13
- Specific tumor treatment method: ablation, cell therapy
- Neurological disease: Parkinson's
- First imaging diagnosis time: 2020-08-27json results:

Project name: Solid tumor 199 gene detection

Detection technology:
1. Target region capture + high-throughput sequencing
2. Immunohistochemistry (IHC)

Detection content:
Solid tumor 199 Genetic testing covers 199 genes and indicators that are closely related to cancer targeting, immunity and chemotherapy. Through target region capture and high-throughput sequencing methods, cancer-related gene mutations, amplifications and fusions are comprehensively and accurately evaluated. In addition, immunohistochemistry (IHC) methods are used to evaluate the sensitivity of anti-angiogenic drugs and immune drugs.

Test results:
1. TP53 p.E258D: sensitive
2. EGFR copy number variation: sensitive
3. ERBB2 copy number variation: sensitive
4. EGFR p.L858R: sensitive
5. PARP inhibitor: HRD negative, may be insensitive
6. Microsatellite instability (MSI): microsatellite stable (MSS), low benefit
7. PD-L1 expression: positive
8. DNA damage repair: pathogenic mutations detected in the DDR pathway
9. Hereditary syndrome: not detected, average risk
10. Overall sample quality: Grade A

Special information for genetic testing:
- KRAS G12D
- FGFR positive
- Immunoassay date: 2021-11-19
- MET gene contains MET14 insertion variation

Clinical tips:
This test shows that PD-L1 expression is positive, and the patient may have a good response to immunotherapy. At the same time, the presence of EGFR and ERBB2 variations also suggests that targeted therapy may bring higher efficacy. Pathogenic mutations in the DNA damage repair pathway indicate sensitivity to specific chemotherapy drugs. It is necessary to combine the patient's clinical information and test results to develop an individualized treatment plan for the patient.

Current medical history: The patient had intermittent cough without obvious cause from March 2023 to March 2024, mainly dry cough, occasionally coughing up a little white sputum, no fever, no hemoptysis, no chest pain, coughing at night, no fatigue, no muscle aches and joint pains. Our outpatient clinic performed a complete lung CT scan, which showed: space-occupying lesions in the left lower lung, with multiple nodules in both lungs and space-occupying left adrenal glands. Routine blood tests showed no abnormalities. The patient visited the respiratory department of our hospital for a fiber bronchial pathological biopsy. The pathological results showed: adenocarcinoma in the basal segment of the left lower lobe of the lung. The results of the gene test showed: KRAS G12A STK11 mutation. The PET-CT examination of our hospital showed: the FDG metabolism of the space-occupying lesions in the left lower lobe of the lung was significantly increased, which was consistent with the characteristics of malignant transformation. The possibility of left lung cancer was considered high. Please combine the clinical and pathological results. The FDG metabolism of multiple nodules in both lungs was abnormally increased, and multiple metastatic tumors in both lungs were considered. The left adrenal mass was accompanied by a mild increase in FDG metabolism, and follow-up was recommended. The bilateral cervical lymph nodes were enlarged, and some FDG metabolism was increased; the FDG metabolism of the small lymph nodes in the mediastinum, bilateral axilla, pelvic cavity, and bilateral inguinal area was not significantly increased, combined with clinical findings. On 2023-04-20 and 2023-05-11, the patient received pemetrexed 0.8g ivgtt + carboplatin injection 500mg ivgtt + bevacizumab 700 ivgtt q21d in our department. On 2023-05-31, ALT 266 U/L and AST 141U/L were checked at Daqing Traditional Chinese Medicine Hospital. He received liver protection treatment in our hospital. Then, on 2023-06-06, 07-20 and 2023-08-09, the original chemotherapy was continued for 6 cycles, and then bevacizumab maintenance treatment was performed for 2 cycles. The patient's chest CT was re-examined and progressed compared with the previous one. The efficacy evaluation was PD. The patient continued to receive treatment in our outpatient clinic. After reading the relevant information, the outpatient physician admitted the patient to our department with "lung malignant tumor". During the course of the disease, the patient had no dizziness, headache, nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, abdominal distension, abdominal pain, frequent urination, urgency, or pain, no change in urine color, good diet, good sleep, normal bowel movements, and no weight change.
Condition at admission: The patient has no fever, cough, dyspnea, or wheezing, no nausea or vomiting, no headache or dizziness, no frequent urination or urgency, good diet and sleep, and normal urine and stool. Physical examination: The general condition is good, the mind is clear and the speech is clear, the nutrition is moderate, and there is no appearance of anemia. The abdomen is flat and soft, no gastrointestinal pattern or peristaltic waves are seen, no tenderness or rebound pain, the liver and spleen are not palpable, and the bowel sounds are 5 times/min. Both upper limbs are well mobile, without clubbing
fingers and toes, and both lower limbs are free of edema. Knee tendon reflexes and abdominal wall reflexes are symmetrical, and Klinefelter's sign and Babinski's sign are negative.

Diagnosis upon admission: Lung malignancy T3NOM1 IV stage secondary lung malignancy

Treatment process: The patient's condition has progressed. According to the NCCN guidelines, second-line treatment can be given with docetaxel monotherapy. The patient's blood routine and liver and kidney function

have no obvious contraindications. Today, docetaxel 120mg ivgtt chemotherapy is given, supplemented with antiemetic, stomach protection and other relief treatments. The patient's treatment is over and he is discharged today.

Admission and discharge records:
The patient is a female, 44 years old, from Laoshan, and was sent for examination by Laoshan Thoracic Surgery. The date of submission is 2023-02-28, and the doctor who submitted the examination is Wang Yongjie. The ward is Laoshan Thoracic Surgery Ward. The materials submitted for examination include the posterior basal segment of the right lower lobe and the right upper lobe. The clinical diagnosis is pulmonary space-occupying lesions.

Gross examination showed that the posterior basal segment of the right lower lobe showed micro-invasive adenocarcinoma, with a diameter of about 0.5cm, without involving the lung stump. The posterior segment of the right upper lobe showed poorly differentiated invasive adenocarcinoma, about 2*1.5*1cm in size, with complex glandular structures and solid types. Airway dissemination is positive, and the bronchial stump is not invaded.

The pathological diagnosis is micro-invasive adenocarcinoma in the posterior basal segment of the right lower lobe, and poorly differentiated invasive adenocarcinoma in the posterior segment of the right upper lobe. Immunohistochemistry results showed that TTF-1 and NapsinA were positive, and CK5/6 and P40 were negative. Special staining results showed no visceral pleural invasion.

According to medical data such as ALK translocation, TP53 positivity, and HER4 positivity, the patient started treatment on 2020-01-01, was first diagnosed by imaging on 2020-03-10, underwent the first lung resection on 2021-05-11, and had brain metastasis on 2022-01-07. The specific treatment for the tumor is thoracic perfusion. In addition, the patient also has complications such as hyperuricemia. The ECOG date is 2021-06-11, and the NTRK gene has been rearranged. ##Medical Data##. The reporting doctor is Wang Wenhong. The initial doctor is Wang Wenhong. JSON results:

Peking University Cancer Hospital
Beijing Cancer Hospital
(10,000th
Medical Record
Date: 2023-10-26
Medical Record Number: 10312
Department: Outpatient Department of Thoracic Oncology
Main complaint: right lung cancer, brain metastasis
Current medical history: In January 2013, right lung adenocarcinoma was diagnosed, the tumor size was about 2*2*3 cm, mainly acinar, and partially adherent. 0/9 in area 4, 0/4 in area 7, 0/3 in area 11, Ki67 expression was less than 2%. After surgery, he received ritoprazole-platinum chemotherapy for a total of 4 cycles, and the metabolism of mediastinal lymph nodes increased slightly during treatment. Further treatment included targeted and pericardial perfusion.
In 2020, the patient reported that his condition worsened and he received ritoprazole*8 cycles, OPDIVO*8 cycles, the specific end time is February 2021; no obvious mutations were found in genetic testing.
After taking the oral targeted drug anlotinib in July 2021, symptoms of decreased appetite and hypertension appeared.
A reexamination in October 2021 showed that multiple nodules were newly found in both brains, which were considered to be metastases. On October 10 of the same year, chest CT showed that multiple metastases in both lungs and pleura had progressed, with bilateral pleural thickening and enlarged lymph nodes in the mediastinum. There were high-density shadows inside thoracic vertebrae 8 and thoracic vertebrae 10, with no significant changes compared with the previous ones.
On November 5, 2021, blood gene testing was performed again, and EGFR19 deletion mutations were found. HER3 (ERBB3) is positive, STK11 is positive.
On December 9, 2021, osimertinib was used for treatment for one month, and on December 7 The chest CT scan (No. 216921) reviewed on August 13 showed changes after right lower lobectomy. There were patchy and nodular fuzzy shadows in both lungs, which were consistent with changes after metastatic treatment. Bullae appeared in both lungs, and the 7th group of mediastinal lymph nodes were slightly larger (17x9mm), which required further investigation. A small amount of pleural effusion on the right side. Multiple bone metastases need to be analyzed in combination with bone scan. The head MRI showed multiple brain metastases, some of which may be accompanied by bleeding, and the efficacy was assessed as partial remission (PR).
On August 13, 2022, the brain enhanced MRI showed that most of the multiple brain metastases disappeared, and the remaining small enhancement foci did not change much compared with the previous time. On August 22, the comprehensive abdominal ultrasound showed that there was no significant change in the bilateral supraclavicular lymph nodes compared with May 18, 2022. The results of the chest enhanced CT scan reviewed on August 28 were roughly unchanged compared with the previous time.
On November 30, 2022, the condition was stable.
On February 19, 2023, the chest enhanced CT was re-examined, and the results showed changes after right lower lobectomy. The metastasis of both lungs was partially the same as before and partially enlarged. The 7th group of mediastinal lymph nodes remained unchanged compared with the previous time. The small amount of pleural effusion on the right side was the same as the previous time, and the multiple bone metastases did not change. On February 21, the enhanced MRI of the head showed that the left frontal lobe enhancement focus was unclear this time, and the suspicious enhancement focus on the right temporal lobe was smaller and less enhanced than before. The ultrasound reexamination on February 23 showed multiple lymph nodes in both supraclavicular regions, which did not change significantly compared with November 16, 2022.

### New admission and discharge records

Date: 2023-02-23

Department: Radiotherapy Outpatient

Main complaint: Diagnosed with squamous cell lung cancer for 2 years, with multiple metastases

Current medical history:

The patient underwent surgery for adenosquamous carcinoma of the left upper lobe on March 2, 2020, and received 6 cycles of chemotherapy with carboplatin and lobaplatin after surgery. Later, due to enlarged mediastinal lymph nodes, 6 cycles of chemotherapy with carboplatin combined with pemetrexed were performed, and radiotherapy was received at a local hospital. On January 11, 2022, multiple new solid nodules were found in both lungs, and the larger ones were large and small metastases to be excluded; no signs of metastasis were found in the brain. The results of genetic testing are as follows:

* EGFR20 insertion mutation, STK11 positive
* ROS1 positive
* BRCA2 mutation
* KRAS amplification

Radiation pneumonia, reexamination after hormone treatment, chest enhanced CT showed that the solid nodules in both lungs were partially reduced and disappeared, and a small amount of pleural effusion on the left side was reduced.

MDT recommended reexamination of chest CT, and the possibility of a second primary was not excluded. Currently, hormone therapy is being continued and the dosage is being gradually reduced. Chaoyang Hospital reexamination of ECT: metastasis to the 4th, 6th-9th and 5th and 7th ribs on the left side is not excluded. The patient has no history of trauma. Occasionally, there is chest pain, which is obvious when turning over at night. Hormone reduction and discontinuation today.

Four months after the end of radiotherapy, no targeted therapy was performed. Our hospital's PET-CT showed:

* After surgery on the left upper lobe, no high metabolism was found in the bronchial stump. Multiple micro and small nodules in both lungs, none of which showed high metabolism, and follow-up is recommended. Multiple inflammatory strip shadows in both lungs. A small amount of effusion in the left pleural cavity.
* Multiple rib fractures, some with high metabolism, please combine clinical conditions. Consult the condition.

The patient was admitted to the hospital for treatment today, and the following treatment was given after examination:

* Routine three-month reexamination, close follow-up of the left cerebellar hemisphere nodules, reexamination of brain MRI if necessary, and follow-up for discomfort.
* Scattered small nodules in both lungs are the same as before. Fractures of some ribs on both sides are the same as before.
* Bilateral supraclavicular lymph node color Doppler ultrasound examination

Past history: Gout (endocrine and immune system disease)

Family history: None

Physical examination: No special abnormalities found.

Outpatient diagnosis: Squamous cell carcinoma of the lung (postoperative, left upper lobe adenosquamous carcinoma, bilateral lung metastasis?), bone metastasis (to be investigated)

Treatment: Provide appropriate treatment and care according to the condition, and closely observe changes in the condition. Routine three-month review of chest CT and bilateral supraclavicular lymph node color Doppler ultrasound, follow-up if discomfort occurs.

Occupation:

Main complaint: Diagnosed with left lung cancer for more than 1 year, after multiple courses of treatment.
Medical history narrator:
Patient himself
Admission: Intermittent needle-like pain in the left chest appeared in early September 2020, the degree was mild, did not affect sleep and rest, no fever, sputum blood, chest pain, etc., the pain worsened at the end of November 2020, and the local hospital for chest CT examination showed solid nodules in the left upper lobe of the lung and diffuse small nodules in both lungs. On December 4, PET-CT was performed in our hospital. It showed: a solid mass in the left upper lobe of the lung, with increased metabolism, which was considered lung cancer; multiple nodules in the remaining lungs and pleural surfaces, with increased metabolism, which was considered intrapulmonary metastasis; multiple thickening of the left pleura with increased metabolism, which was considered metastasis; increased metabolism in the strip of the left lower chest wall, the nature of which was to be determined; multiple lymph nodes in the mediastinum (4L, 5, 7 areas), left hilum, and left pericardial septum group, with increased metabolism, which was considered metastasis; small lymph nodes in the mediastinum 4R, 6 areas, with mild increased metabolism, tended to be mainly granulomatous lesions; bilateral lock Small lymph nodes scattered in the supraspinal area and deep cervical group, some with mild metabolic activity, the nature of which is to be determined; metastasis to the left 6th rib and lumbar vertebral body, with increased metabolism; local density of the sacrum decreased, focal metabolism increased in the right wing of the sacrum, also tending to be metastatic; diffuse metabolism increased in the middle and upper rectum, poor local intestinal expansion, slight thickening of the intestinal wall, the nature of which is to be determined; multiple lymph nodes around the intestinal tract and in the presacral area, with mild increased metabolism, the nature of which is to be determined; multiple lymph nodes in the retroperitoneum, some with mild increased metabolism; thickening of the left adrenal medial branch, with increased metabolism, alert for metastasis. Color Doppler ultrasound-guided puncture of the left supraclavicular lymph node; pathology showed a small number of adenocarcinoma cells, but immunohistochemistry and gene testing were not performed due to too little tissue. Adenocarcinoma cells were found in the left upper lobe of the lung under CT guidance. On December 14, 2020 and January 4, 2021, the first and second cycles of pemetrexed + cisplatin chemotherapy were given respectively (drugs: pemetrexed 1.0g, did cisplatin 50mg, d1-3, Q21d), and zoledronic acid treatment was given. Genetic testing report on January 4, 2021: showed deletion mutation of exon 19 of EGFR gene. On January 28, 2021, due to the epidemic, he was unable to return to Beijing, and received the third cycle of pemetrexed + cisplatin chemotherapy (specifically pemetrexed disodium 1000mg d1 + cisplatin 50mg d1-3) at a local hospital, and began oral targeted therapy with icotinib hydrochloride (125mg tid) on February 5. On March 26, 2021, our hospital received the fourth cycle of pemetrexed + cisplatin chemotherapy (specifically pemetrexed disodium 1000mg d1 + cisplatin 50mg d1-3 treatment), and gave zoledronic acid treatment, with no obvious discomfort. The efficacy evaluation of 3 cycles was PR. On March 19, 2021, our hospital received the fifth cycle of treatment: pemetrexed disodium 10g, d1\
cisplatin 50mg, d1-3, ivgtt, Q21, icotinib 125mg tid, and zoledronic acid treatment. After chemotherapy, the patient had obvious vomiting and fatigue, and grade III bone marrow suppression, which improved after symptomatic treatment and maintained PR after 5 cycles of efficacy evaluation. The patient had bilateral lower limb eyelid edema after angiography, and improved after anti-allergic treatment. The patient had obvious nausea and vomiting after 5 cycles of chemotherapy. On April 13, 2021, the sixth cycle of chemotherapy was performed, and the regimen was adjusted to: pemetrexed disodium 1.0g, d1+carboplatin 500mg, d2, ivgtt, Q21, icotinib 125mg tid. The patient still complained of obvious nausea and vomiting after 6 cycles of chemotherapy, so the regimen was adjusted on May 7, 2021: pemetrexed disodium lg, ivgtt, d1, Q21d, icotinib 125mg, po, tid/Q3W. On May 28, 2021, the original regimen was continued for the eighth cycle of treatment. On June 25, 2021, targeted therapy with bevacizumab combined with icotinib was started: bevacizumab 500mg
ivgtt d1/Q28d + icotinib 125mg tid. The efficacy of the CT reexamination during the period was evaluated as SD. On June 14, 2022, CT: solid tumor in the left upper lobe of the lung, with extremely irregular shape, some layers are slightly fuller than before, and the current size is about 3.4×2.4cm, with multiple burrs on the edge and more blurred than before, and multiple metastatic nodules in both lungs and pleura, some of which are similar to the previous ones, and some are slightly larger than before. Considering the progression of the disease, the treatment plan was changed on June 20, 2022: pemetrexed 1.0g dl + bevacizumab 500mg dl + osimertinib 80mg qd po/Q28d. On February 10, 2023, it was adjusted to pemetrexed + anlotinib + osimertinib treatment. On May 9, 2023, pemetrexed was suspended due to intolerance of adverse reactions. On July 10, 2023, the treatment plan was adjusted to bevacizumab + albumin paclitaxel + carboplatin. Past medical history: denied hepatitis, tuberculosis and other infectious diseases, denied hypertension, coronary heart disease, diabetes and other chronic diseases, denied

The patient first visited our hospital on 2020-05-14. Due to the discovery of a nodule in the left lower lobe of the lung, after detailed examination and comparison of old films, it was diagnosed as limited stage small cell lung carcinoma with mediastinal lymph node metastasis. The head MRI on September 15, 2022 showed localized thickening and enhancement of the left frontal lobe meninges, which was considered to be meningeal metastasis. On September 19, 2022, abdominal CT found multiple nodules in bilateral adrenal glands, which needed to be further distinguished from metastasis and adenoma. Ultrasound examination on September 14, 2022 found multiple lymph nodes in both necks.

On September 23, 2022, the PET/CT examination results showed a high-density strip shadow in the lateral basal segment of the right lower lobe of the lung, with no metabolic activity; solid nodules in the left lower lobe of the lung were only accompanied by mild metabolism; there were slightly low-density nodules in the right lobe of the thyroid gland, with mild metabolism; small inflammatory lymph nodes in the bilateral neck, a small amount of pericardial effusion, mesenteric fat membrane inflammation and spinal degeneration. Lumbar puncture performed in an external hospital showed intracranial pressure of 115, cerebrospinal fluid protein quantification of 0.582g/L (increased), and both adenosine dehydrogenase and lactate dehydrogenase were reduced.

The left lung puncture pathology report performed by the China-Japan Friendship Hospital on October 13, 2022 showed fibrous and striated muscle tissue, a small amount of lung tissue, accompanied by hyperplastic fibrous connective tissue and necrosis, while no abnormal cells were found in cerebrospinal fluid cytology. On October 26, 2022, the patient received brain prophylactic irradiation, and the radiotherapy plan was 25Gy for the whole brain in 10 fractions.

On November 3, 2022, the patient underwent the seventh radiotherapy and complained of increased vomiting. After the radiotherapy ended on November 8, 2022, the vomiting was relieved by the application of granisetron transdermal patch. On November 10, 2022, a PICC extubation examination was performed. On March 14, 2023, the patient completed 5 sessions of Infifan immune maintenance therapy, with an adverse reaction of hypothyroidism, and no abnormalities were found in the chest reexamination at the local hospital.

Subsequently, the Department of Thoracic Surgery was consulted and decided to perform ultrasound combined with CT-guided puncture biopsy of the left lung space. During the biopsy, 50mL of hemoptysis occurred, and there was a slight sense of shortness of breath. After treatment, the symptoms were relieved. Considering the high possibility of left lung adenocarcinoma, ametinib targeted therapy was given while waiting for the pathological results and genetic test results. The patient and his family requested to recuperate outside the hospital. With the consent of the superior physician, he was discharged today.
Medical data: The nervous system disease is peripheral neuritis, and the circulatory system disease is hyperlipidemia. The first pathological diagnosis of thymic carcinoma was 2020-11-06, ALK amplification, BRCA is BRCA2, KRAS is G12V, and HER4 (ERBB4) is positive. The brain metastasis site is secondary brain malignant tumor, and there is no brain metastasis. The pathological date is 2021-08-16.

Discharge status: The patient's mental state is good, hemoptysis has not occurred again, and the pain in the left lower limb still exists. Lofencodeine sustained-release tablets (20mg 2/day) are given for analgesic symptomatic treatment.

Discharge diagnosis: Left lung adenocarcinoma stage IV, multiple lymph node metastasis, left pleural metastasis, left pleural effusion, multiple bone metastasis, bilateral lung metastasis is not excluded.

Discharge instructions:
1. Pay attention to rest, avoid colds, and take medicine as prescribed: 2 tablets of fenpropiform sustained-release tablets 2/day; 2 tablets of ametinib mesylate tablets/day; 2 tablets of Yunnan Baiyao capsules 4/day; 1 tablet of moxifloxacin hydrochloride tablets (Bayer)/day.
2. Closely observe adverse reactions such as rashes, and contact the attending physician in time if any abnormalities occur.

Complaint: Diagnosis of right lung cancer stage IV bone metastasis for nearly 7 years, brain metastasis found for more than 3 years targeted treatment
Medical History: 2016-9 Coughing and hemoptysis right lung mass, weight loss, 3 gray-white tissues in the right upper lobe, diameter 0.2cm. It is consistent with lung adenocarcinoma, with solid growth as the main pattern. High metabolic mass in the right upper lobe, growing across the oblique fissure, considered to be lung cancer, and with distal atelectasis. Multiple lymph node metastases in the mediastinum 2R, 3P, and 7 groups, right pleural effusion. 2016-10-14 Gene 21 exon mutation is reliable for treatment with chemisulfite. 2016-11-18 After one month of treatment with Camena, the tumor shrank from 6 cm to 4 cm, and liver function was poor. 2018-5-16 Bone metastasis treatment, continued targeted therapy. 2018-5-30 No new lesions were found in the reexamination. 2019-06-21 Chest CT in our hospital: 1. The soft tissue mass in the right upper lobe of the lung is slightly larger than before. 2. A small amount of pleural effusion on the right side is slightly less than before. 3. Multiple bone destruction in the thoracic spine and bilateral ribs is the same as before, and metastasis is considered. 4. Multiple low-enhancement nodules in the spleen are similar to the previous ones: the tumor marker CEA is slightly higher than before, CA125 and CA199 are slightly lower than before, and the patient's general condition is good, and no discomfort symptoms occur. 2019-07-09 Brain MRI was performed at Beijing Chaoyang Hospital Affiliated to Capital Medical University, showing: lesions in the right parietal lobe and left frontal lobe. Combined with the medical history, metastasis is considered: ischemic lesions in the cortical and medullary junction of the bilateral frontal and parietal lobes. 2019-12-25 Local radiotherapy started in the radiotherapy department. SBRT
95%PGTV1, 3D expansion of left frontal lobe lesion 3mm, 28Gy/2 times: 95%PGTV2, 3D expansion of right occipital lobe lesion 3mm, 52Gy/8 times. 2020-106 Brain radiotherapy ended, 2020/4/17 CT: 1. Cancer in the right upper lobe, a small amount of pleural effusion on the right side, and scattered small patchy nodules in both lungs, all of which were not significantly changed compared with the previous changes. 2. Multiple metastatic destruction of thoracic spine and bilateral ribs, some of which were new, 4. Diffuse low-density lesions in the spleen were scanned. 2020/4/21 Head MR: The structures of the bilateral cerebral hemispheres are roughly symmetrical, and double abnormal enhancement nodules are seen in the left frontal lobe and right parietal lobe, about 6mm, and flake edema areas can be seen around the lesions. Double abnormal enhancement nodules in the left frontal lobe and right parietal lobe with edema, metastasis is considered. 2020-08-27 Brain MRI: The metastatic nodules in the left frontal lobe and right parietal lobe are slightly enlarged (increased by 2mm), and the peritumoral edema is slightly more obvious than before. The right frontal lobe enhancement focus is also considered a metastatic focus. 2020/11/30 Brain MRI: Compared with the 2020-08-27 head MR: The bilateral frontal lobes and right parietal lobes have double abnormal enhancement nodules that have enlarged. The larger one was about 8mm and is now about 9x7mm: 2021/6/15 Brain MRI: Multiple brain metastases have enlarged, and the range of peritumoral edema has increased. The patient has no symptoms related to brain metastasis, and the patient refuses to actively undergo anti-tumor treatment. 2021-10-18 Head CT showed that multiple brain metastases have increased, and the range of peritumoral edema has increased. Chest CT showed that the right upper lobe mass was the same as before, and a small amount of pleural effusion on the right side was the same as before. The scattered nodules in both lungs are roughly the same as before: multiple bone metastases and pathological compression fractures of thoracic vertebrae 5-7 are the same as before. Multiple nodular high-density lesions beside the right humeral head are the same as before. Splenic diffuse low-density lesions were also scanned: 2023-2 re-examination of the head MRI showed that the brain edema was aggravated compared with the previous one. 2023-04-28 re-examination and evaluation of efficacy: brain lesions were reduced: 2023-08-04 outpatient evaluation: brain edema worsened, right lung pleural effusion increased. Continue to take oral vometinib until now. 2023-10-17 re-examination of chest enhanced CT and brain MRI showed that the lung and brain lesions were unchanged compared with the previous one.

Chief Complaint: Diagnosed with left lung cancer for more than 1 month
Current Medical History: The patient is currently hospitalized in the orthopedics department of our hospital. In December 2022, he developed low back pain. Later, due to the aggravation of pain, he went to the Affiliated Hospital of Inner Mongolia Medical University for treatment. Lumbar CT and lumbar MRI examinations showed multiple abnormal signals in the thoracic, lumbar and sacral vertebrae, which were considered to be metastatic diseases, and myeloma could not be ruled out; chest CT examination showed peripheral cancer in the upper lobe of the left lung, accompanied by mediastinal lymph nodes, bilateral lungs and bone metastases. On August 27, 2023, spinal neurolysis was performed in the Department of Orthopedics of our hospital, and the pathological diagnosis was completed: combined with histology and immunohistochemistry, it was believed that the tumor cells originated from the lungs. TPS <1%. The abundance of EGFR L861Q mutation was 37.9%, accompanied by TP53 mutation and increased CDK copy number. The patient was in poor general condition, with loss of appetite and poor nutritional status. In mid-September, oral afatinib 40mg was started for 5 days. Due to poor tolerance of persistent diarrhea, it was adjusted to 30mg. After 2 days, it was discontinued due to severe diarrhea. Suvotinib was switched to treatment according to the doctor's advice. Currently receiving supportive treatment.
Past history: Respiratory diseases include chronic bronchitis and pneumothorax. Neurological diseases include schizophrenia. The first pathological diagnosis was confirmed on January 20, 2019. The pathological date was May 27, 2021, and the record date was August 15, 2021. The first lung resection surgery was on December 13, 2021. HER3 (ERBB3) expression is positive.

Peking University Cancer Hospital
Beijing Cancer Hospital
(Page 1)
Medical Record
Date: 2023-10-10
Department: Outpatient Department of Thoracic Oncology
Main complaint: Postoperative right lung cancer
Current medical history:
On 2018-02-12, right upper lobectomy was performed. Postoperative pathology showed peripheral invasive adenocarcinoma, some of which were wall-attached, some were mucinous adenocarcinoma, and a small part was micropapillary carcinoma. The tumor did not invade the pulmonary membrane, no cancer cells were found in the incision, and there was no lymph node metastasis. The postoperative stage was T1N1M0. Immunohistochemistry results showed: ALK(-), HER-1(+), HER-2(+).
Postoperative gene testing showed EGFR21 exon mutation, and TMB was 9.35 Muts/Mb.
On 2018-03-20, chemotherapy with pemetrexed combined with cisplatin was started, and a total of 4 courses of treatment were performed. The last chemotherapy was performed on 2018-05-31.
2018-12 CT results of the reexamination showed that the postoperative condition of the right lung cancer was similar to that on 2018-09-04. The lymph nodes in the mediastinum were enlarged, which was considered to be metastasis.
2018-12-04 Icotinib was started and stopped on 2019-01-01.
2018-12-28 Positioning was performed today. The plan type is VMAT, and the target range and dose include 60Gy/30 times for the metastatic lymph nodes in the 95%PGTV4R area, 60Gy/30 times for the 95%PTV2R, 4R, 7 areas, and the right hilar lymph node drainage area. At the same time, 2 cycles of PP chemotherapy were performed. Icotinib was discontinued. 29 radiotherapy sessions were completed on 2019-02-12, and the patient had no adverse reactions reported.
2019-06-25 Four months after the end of radiotherapy, the local hospital brain MRI (-), chest and abdominal enhanced CT and B-ultrasound bone scan showed no signs of tumor recurrence.
2021-04-22, 2 years and 3 months after the end of radiotherapy, the abdominal CT was re-examined and no new lesions were found. Neck ultrasound showed diffuse lesions and multiple nodules in both thyroid glands, which tended to be benign. Chest CT results have not yet been returned.
The patient had a history of psoriasis and lupus nephritis, had suffered from infectious diseases such as hepatitis A, and had eye, ear, nose and throat related bleeding events. At the same time, there was a history of breast cancer and renal anemia. In terms of genetic markers, STK11 and TP53 were both positive, BRAF was non-V600, MET showed MET14 deletion, HER4 (ERBB4) was positive, ALK was amplified, and ROS1 was rearranged.
2023-01-03 Nearly 4 years after the end of radiotherapy, 2022-11 external hospital reexamination chest CT: irregular nodule shadows were found in the space between the trachea and the posterior vena cava, about 23 mm long. No metastasis was found in the head MRI and cervical lymph node ultrasound.
2023-05-23 4 years and 3 months after the end of radiotherapy, this comprehensive reexamination in our hospital, chest CT: compared with the CT in our hospital on 2021-10, the left supraclavicular and mediastinal lymph nodes were enlarged, and metastasis was considered.
Doctor: Zhao Jun json result:

2021-02-20 Back pain, cough. Now taking osimertinib orally every other day. Spinal radiotherapy was performed at Guang'anmen Hospital, and bone cementing surgery is planned.

Focus, continue to track; multiple bone metastases increased in density, some surrounding soft tissue tumors increased, and some vertebral pathological compression fractures; left lung micronodules and old lesions in the left lung were the same
as before.

2023-7-12 Online, oral megestrol, appetite but only stomach discomfort, frequent vomiting, dry noise, and sometimes foot swelling.

Clinical diagnosis: right lung squamous cell carcinoma
Admission: The patient had symptoms of cough, sputum, hemoptysis, shortness of breath, and body temperature of 37.5℃ when admitted to the hospital. Physical examination: dry rales were heard in the right lung, and vital signs were stable.
Diagnosis and treatment process: After admission, relevant examinations were completed, including CT, PET-CT and other examinations, and it was confirmed that it was right lung squamous cell carcinoma, and the clinical stage was stage III. According to the tumor gene test results, ROS1 was positive, so the next treatment plan was considered to include targeted therapy for ROS1.
Treatment process: The patient began to use nasogastric feeding treatment on 2020-12-15, and was diagnosed with digestive system diseases on 2020-04-07, and underwent surgery and organ transplantation. HER2 (ERBB2) insertion mutation has been confirmed, and the KRAS gene mutation is G12A.
Discharge: After comprehensive treatment, the patient's symptoms improved, shortness of breath was significantly relieved, body temperature returned to normal, and there were no symptoms such as cough and sputum.
